input_text:
Categories: Southern, Barbeque, Restaurants | Review: Service can be slow if there are only 2 people working the line, especially since the person you order the meats from has the unenviable task of quickly weighing the meats. 

Anyway, I think the food is good- much better than other BBQ I have tried in the area. I have always gotten the fancy pork sandwich and then added a cup of the original BBQ sauce on top since it's a bit bland without it. If you don't mind waiting about 10-15 minutes for your food, it's worth it. Each meal comes with free ice cream, if that's a draw for you. 

One thing that bothers me is that they only offer reusable cups for drinks. We've been here several times and keep forgetting our cups so we now have a whole stash at home.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Good meat, great salsa. Ordered two Asada tacos, they were Yoooooge! Meat was well seasoned, I don't like beans, but they went well with the meat. Wife had a carnitas burrito, she loved it. Great spot for fast and delicious Mexican food.

Categories: Nightlife, Breweries, Food, Bars, Beer Bar | Review: Great local edition to the emerging Goleta Beer scene. The porter and blonde are good quality beers. Parking is a little tough so uber or be prepared to walk!

Categories: American (New), Tapas/Small Plates, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants, Burgers | Review: This is a great place, with good food and good ambience. The Tri-tip sandwich is out of this world. Would definitely recommend the wings and the bacon bites as a side. The patio area, music and friendly ambience make this place a must-visit in Santa Barbara.

Categories: Nightlife, American (Traditional), Cafes, Restaurants, Wine Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, Bars | Review: Good food, good price! Place is always full on the weekends but it's not that long of a wait. Staff is amazing and they have a bar.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Maybe they changed chefs when they re-opened? We used to come here for special occasions and were willing to pay ridiculous prices for simple food because the setting is so beautiful and the service has always been professional -  but we went the other day for our anniversary (42nd!) and the food was just mediocre and even more expensive than in pre-flood days (fish and chips is now almost $40 and the ribs even more). So you can come here and drop $180 (with tip) for two entrees, no app and no desert or you can go somewhere else and get as good (or better) a meal at half the price. We were the only diners until after 7PM so it looks like others feel the same way - we will not go back.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I ordered the Korean spare ribs. I was disappointed by the quantity of meat on the ribs, there was only five or six bites of meat in the whole meal and it was about $20. I would say that the prices are expensive based on the quantity of food. I thought the sauces in the dish had very good flavor and taste. However, the ribs were burnt to a crisp and tasted ashy. I really like the atmosphere in the restaurant and the fact that they had patio seating. I might go there again but I wouldn't order the same dish. I wouldn't consider this restaurant to be a authentic Vietnamese/Asian food.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Pros:  Delicious Food, Friendly Staff, Beautiful Clean Modern Setting
Cons:  Price, Quantity

How much does a meal cost here?  

$10-15 for a snack/appetizer sized portion.  A dent on your appetite.
$25 for a filling meal (not stuffed), without beverage.
$30-32 for a filling meal, with a single alcoholic beverage.

Pros:  Delicious Food, Friendly Staff, Beautiful Clean Modern Settingne was the best.  And then order a side of noodles, rice, or something like their kung pao cauliflower, which was about 1/20th a head of cauliflower in a pool of delicious sauce (worth getting if you like cauliflower!).   Or 3 baos  and then sharing/splitting a side dish.

Even then you won't be stuffed.

It's great, delicious food.  Perfectly cooked and delivered hot to your seat.

But I think you have to go in with the above $ prices in mind, because there simply is no way to spend $10-12 here and not feel like you just got a TASTE of a menu.

I absolutely enjoyed dining here, and wish them the best with their new location!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: First visit to the new fancy revamped Rudy's. 

While I actually prefer the casual, walk-up counter ordering at its previous incarnation, the interior now is modern, spiffy, sharp, but not even remotely "Mexican." 

The good news is there are more seats, and obviously, table service which comes with free chips, yay!

Bad news is, the servers are kind of annoying with their black outfits and someone needs to remind them that we're in suburban little Goleta, and not some trendy big city joint. So leave the attitude, and just be friendly and warm for goodness sake.

Prices seem to have gone up on some items only.

Chile relleno plate was awesomely good, with the pasilla pepper being the size of my hand!

Will let you know if the chimichanga has improved any (at the old Rudys, it was a disaster). 

This new place has lots of potential, but I hope the prices don't increase too much with the rent.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Great spot for craft cocktails! Good ambiance and quietly tucked away on a side street at the end of the busy state street in downtown santa barbara. A group of us were spending the weekend in SB and this was our first drinks stop for the night and we couldn't have been happier. Every drink was uniquely crafted, refreshing and smooth. Ginspiration point is a must try - you won't be disappointed. We also got midnight in the garden, spicy cucumber margarita and the gimlet. There were quite a few other interesting drinks on the menu, will be back next time for more.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: Try the paella. I've had paella in Spain and let me tell you, this one has nothing on them

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: Horrible service!!!!!!
The waiter kept forgetting our drinks and we didn't receive the right orders nor did some people in our party received their order at all. I use to love coming here but the staff is very unorganized.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Coffee Roasteries | Review: Great coffee shop in perfect SB location right on State Street. We had soy lattes and lemonades and all were delicious. Service was also excellent. If you like coffee definitely go to The French Press.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Nightlife, Restaurants, Seafood, Cafes | Review: I honestly had not been here for many many years, since I was a little kid. As far as I could tell the place hadn't changed much, but the service was still good, the drinks were awesome (and strong, no complaints there) and the appetizers we had were very good. The bartender could have had a little more personality but hey, can't ask for everything right?!

Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: Really good home brewed beers and craft beers I should say. the western burger is a phenomenal sandwich it was really good. next time I'm going to try some appetizers too next time to go along with it. The staff is very friendly and helpful as well.  Window seats are great for people watching in downtown Santa Barbara
this is the second time I have been here and the foos is on point both times.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Tex-Mex, American (Traditional), Burgers, Food, Sandwiches, Mexican, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: Yum! This was a great find in Santa Barbara, and everything was delicious and fresh.

The site next door is currently under construction and they have a small juice bar set up. On Sat evening they were offering juice tasting to passerby's. The cafe has an outdoor seating area for those who love to enjoy the CA weather. 

You grab a menu when you enter and go into the restaurant to the back to order. The lady at the counter had not tried much of the menu so she was unable to suggest between two options. They give you a number and bring out your food about 5-10 minutes later. It was yummy and in sufficient quantity!

I recommend checking this place out if you're around Santa Barbara and enjoy freshly made food.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: I LOVE EOS!!!!!! My absolute favorite place to dance. I've been there more times then I can count and I keep going back. The speaker system is absolutely nuts, loudest music on the block. The patio area is perfect if you need a break from the loud, though. Sometimes they have two DJ's going, one inside and one outside on the patio. Those are the best nights. Spacious and two separate bars make it easy to get drinks. The bartenders are always awesome, personable, fun, and nice. Every time they put on event it blows my mind away and I have so much fun. Great vibes, will keep going back until I'm too old to be there lol. Best club in Santa Barbara hands down.

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Bubble Tea | Review: Phamous or tasteless cafe?

Either one could fit as the title of this establishment.

The worst Pho I have ever had. It really saddens me because I had really high hopes coming in after reading so many positive reviews. 

I ordered to go and the service itself was impeccable. Nice and courteous, and the food was packed very well. We took a seat and they brought our food out to us.

The soup was bland. Like so bland that if you licked my face after a run I would've been saltier. And that's the thing I feel so salty right now at the lack of salt and flavor.

The meat portions is laughable at best. How could you charge a thirteen dollar bowl of literally noodle and broth with a few strands of meat? That's ridiculous.

Pho in LA costs six dollars and gives at least twice the amount of meat at this establishment. Not only does it taste better but you get more for less.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: This place is AWFUL! service was the worst we have had in a very long time. The food, YUCK!! TERRIBLE. We ordered the lobster ravioli it was fishy, the lobster was wrapped in a piece of frozen dough that looked like a croissant it was doughy, really thick unpalatable. We also ordered the special  which was  bass, scallops in tomato pasta sauce. This dish was supposedly inspired by the chefs trip to Italy. Well, he missed the boat. The scallops were smaller than a dime, frozen the bass maybe 2 pieces frozen. Just TERRIBLE I do not know what this restaurant is thinking. It needs 
a lot of help. Starting with replacing the chef...

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Went on a loud and crazy Sat. Night, just how I like it.  Sat at the bar with two others and we had a night to remember.  Great bartender Greg showed us a real good time.  The food was out of this world, wings, noodles, ahi.  Can't wait to go back.  The drinks we had were on par, looking forward to trying mas.
Categories: Pizza, Italian, Restaurants | Review: I recommend this place where you can feel like you are in home the best service by the owners Thais and Mario! Excellent food

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Do-It-Yourself Food, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Bars | Review: Amazing! Give these people 20 dollars and they will give you a raw piece of steak that you can cook yourself.  What an amazing scam. On top of that this place is dirty as hell.  So confused on why people go here. Bar staff is somewhat unwelcoming as well if you are not over 40.

0.6/5 stars. My brother says try buying a steak at the grocery store
Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Wineries, Food, Arts & Entertainment | Review: Brad is awesome! Very knowledgeable and friendly. I bought the Estate Syrah & 2 Anna's Pinot Noir! Go when Brad is there!

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Very beautiful, cozy, romantic pizzeria. 
I'll try to keep it short and sweet. 
Wait: had a 30 min wait on a Sunday night, no problem though because they take down your cell number and call you when your table is ready. 
Beverages: great wine and beer selections (Italian and local). 
Food: I ordered the 'Greek style' salad- good, not spectacular, but good. For my entree I had the pizza with mozzarella, ham and artichokes. Again, good, not spectacular, but good. 
Price: High prices, but good experience. 
Parking: Lot available out back, first 75 minutes free. 
Sorry, no review on dessert- I'll let you know next time!
Ciao!

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Chile rellenos burrito covered in mole- one of the best things I've ever eaten.

Pupusa: decent, but low filling to masa ratio

Chips with mole sauce and queso fresco: amazing

Horchata: one of the best I've had.

Carne asada burrito covered in mole: Loved everything except for the meat. The meat was very tough.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Okay - the food and drinks are just okay but it's a must-stop and see on your way through SB and up to Santa Ynez. I've been here several times with my family and it's always fun. Walking to the back to see the old jail is cool and the blue jays are very friendly. The staff is wonderful and the service is great! Sitting indoors is also fun, you get the feel of how long it's been around which I enjoy. Definitely stop by if you are on your way up north to do some wine tasting!

Categories: Sandwiches, Salad, Bagels, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Restaurants | Review: I really like Jack's. The coffee is strong and the bagels are the best in the area. Always good for breakfast.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches | Review: I LOVE THIS PLACE!!!! their sandwiches are phenomenal, but the best part are the pickles that are up for grabs.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: I've always enjoyed going to EOS. They have some of the best people working there.  Dj lineups are bomb.com, fans, air conditioning, dance floor inside, and a fireplace out on the patio for cold nights. Enough bathrooms for the ladies so you don't have to wait in line for too long.  It's a place everyone should check out. Shout out to mi amigo out back on the patio Nelson, love ya!

Categories: Active Life, Bowling, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Party & Event Planning, Arcades, Event Planning & Services, Arts & Entertainment | Review: Update!

Zodo's really listens to the reviews they receive. They took action and my complaint was heard and addressed. Went at 8pm last night and it was awesome! Great customer service and fun all around.

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Burgers | Review: Javan's is the best ... I have been going here for a loooong time.  I usually get the chicken club which is super good, but some times I get the philly when I'm not feeling like chicken.  Not really close to authentic Philly style, but still tasty.  The other thing they have going for the them is the fries ... They are AMAZING!!!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: I was so stoked to try this place because the concept is fun and something that Goleta has needed for a while. However the design and service model is terrible. For one, you order in the back of the store and so there was a line huddled near the entrance where no one is allowed to order -- no employee or sign told us this. Secondly, after you order a very expensive ice cream, they put your receipt in a line of other people waiting for their ice cream. We waited over 30 minutes until our name was called... for ice cream. 
The service itself wasn't bad. And the ice cream was good, but in no world is ice cream worth a 30 minute wait for after you have already ordered. Prob won't be coming back anytime soon.

Categories: Pizza, Gluten-Free, Salad, Restaurants, Fast Food | Review: We ordered the pizza online and it was really convenient and simple, I am glad we can add as many as toppings without any extra charges, so we had a total of 17 toppings including two kinds of cheese, 3 kinds of meat and 12 veggies. I also like the fact they don't put the pizza into the oven until the customers show up in store, so they do care about the quality of the food presented to us. For only $8.50, we are so grateful we came here and supported UCSB Public community Fund, we got yummy food and we made a good cause. Thanks Blaze Pizza for your generosity and care about this community, our community!

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Love the food and that cajun martini has made me a fan
Stopped through on my way back to LA from up north a couple of Sunday's ago
No reservations only a party of 2, got to the desk and ended up having to wait about 5 mins while the staff went around and had a toast with all the guest.  Kind of gave me the impression that since we did not make it there prior to the start of the song What A Wonderful World, that we were not important and was just going to have to wait.  They did one by one come and tell us that they would wait on us as soon as the song was over. 
Rude and bad for business

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Restaurants, Food | Review: Service was a little slower than other Peet's but the staff was friendly. No outdoor seating, which was disappointing. The indoor seating is decent with privacy somewhat to each table for working &/or talking. The place is somewhat clean & stocked.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood | Review: This is the best Mexican I've had north of Los Angeles. The service is quick and friendly. The food delicious. We had the chiliquiles, huavos rancheros, and asada tacos. Everyone was completely satisfied, they also sell Mexican coke, yummmaayyy.

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza, Chicken Wings, American (New), Italian | Review: Placed an online order, as what seems to be popular. Anyway nothing special, one large stuff crust pizza.

Arrived early and stayed late, my pizza sat u loved and untouched on a rack with another three or four pizza's. I waited about twenty minutes for them to figure out what to do and just hand me my food. 

The problem is a combination of first time employees, not knowing how to manage customers. Stack orders, dealing with deliveries and manage orders going out as the food is ready, not understanding cashier work and credit cards.

Basically if you'd imagine a restaraunt with no one at the helm and a less then able work force. 

Workers would take the freshest pizzas as they were available to fill orders as needed rather then keep orders together. Resulting in more pizzas being made and the waiting and waiting continues. Cold pizzas end up waiting on the side line and the problems just become exacerbated. 

I ended up going back in addition to this managment issues because low and behold, my pizza was undercooked. Dough hadn't even salidified yet. So another thirty minutes of waiting on one pizza order. 

To top it all off. There is better pizza in town. Cheaper and faster. 

I came here because I hadn't been to a Pizza Hut in fifteen years. I can see why the brand doesn't live strong out here if this is the mindset building out these franchises. 

TL:DR

This place lacks very basic restaraunt managment skills and is staffed with a less then capable staff. Long waits and sub par food. IMO GO ELSEWHERE, saves money and happier experience.

Categories: Soup, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Italian | Review: Solid 4 stars! Don't agree with others here! We were party of 2. Staff was fast, attentive, we got table right away! Drinks are very good quality- ginger Martini is extremely good quality! Recommend it for ladies... Foodwise go for raw beef as appetizer - does keeps its  high standard . Nice view of the street as sun goes down...

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: This review is strictly off of two drinks: a small hot latte and a nitro cold brew

Every time I visit a new coffee shop I always get a hot latte, no additional syrups or flavors added. I feel that this way is the best way for me to gauge how their espresso is and whether it leaves a slight bitter or burned taste or if it's wonderfully smooth. 

Handlebar's latte is the latter: wonderfully smooth. I don't know all the ins and outs of coffee, but a good cup of latte essentially leaves me wanting more and this one did just that. 

Nitro cold brews are also a hit or miss in many places, but it was a hit here. It was smooth as well, instead of tasting like iced coffee. Nitro cold brews are packed with caffeine, or so it feels, so it's not something I would recommend, but it gets the job done here. 

The service was quick and efficient. The line grows fairly long but the staff seem to be a well oiled machine, trying to get customers their drinks as quickly as they can. 

Parking was a little difficult, as we came on a Saturday morning at 9:10am. There are a few pay-by-spot lots and because it's in beautiful Santa Barbara, a walk from a few blocks away doesn't hurt!

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: This is a very cute restaurant. I first noticed it while on a morning walk. I decided to try it this last Sunday around 6:00 pm and joined the waitlist via yelp. When I got to the restaurant I tried to communicate to the hostess that I had added my name to the yelp waitlist, but before I could finish explaining she assumed she knew my question and told me the wait tine was 40 minutes and that yelp is not accurate. I again explained that I had added my name to the waitlist, she rolled her eyes, asked my name and showed me the tablet as she quickly scrolled and said my name wasn't there. This encounter almost made me leave the restaurant, but I decided to give it a shot anyway. Luckily the server assigned to my table was lovely. She had super curly hair and was lively, pleasant and very knowledgeable of the restaurant's menu. The food was okay and the drinks were good. The location is amazing.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: Having a breakfast sandwich from here is a complete meal that will last you until lunch.  We ordered the Egg and Cheese Biscuit with bacon and my daughter ordered the same but gluten free.  The biscuit sandwich was two handfuls of total yummy flavors.  Fresh biscuit with egg covered in a maple glaze and layered with bacon "my my my" it was amazing.  It wasn't very busy at the time but before we left customers were lined up waiting to place their orders.  

The restaurant had plenty of indoor and outdoor seating (the seating is a shared space with the winery next door all under one roof) this was a great start to my day.
While you wait for your order you can watch all the bakers and kitchen staff preparing the food from behind the glass walls of the restaurant.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: I don't know what it is about Mexi Fresh that makes me love it so much. When I used to live on the Mesa I would eat here once a week. At first glance it did not seem like it would be that great. It is located next to a laundromat and always has regaeton blaring. But, that's it charm, I guess.

The Tacos are great (fish, chicken, steak, etc.), and the burritos are quality. But I keep coming back for the tortas. I don't think they are traditional (no guacamole or beans) but that is what I like about them. Plus, the bread they use is unbelievable.

I miss Mexican Fresh, and still go there in occasion when I am in town.

Categories: Sandwiches, Specialty Food, Food, Health Markets, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants | Review: This place has great sandwiches and homemade kombucha! But unfortunately, their fresh made juices were disappointing....mine was FULL of pulp- the last 1/3rd of the cup was dry pulp. And another time I ordered an acai bowl and it still had frozen acai chips intact. Be careful of what you order.
Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: The food was tasteless, overpriced and the quality was horrible. This was def a negative star.

Categories: Gluten-Free, American (New), Restaurants, Sandwiches, Salad, Steakhouses, Delis, American (Traditional) | Review: i had the most amazing tasting tuna melt here it was way too good i would recommend you try it you wont disappointed!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Gracious, friendly service!

Our party of 10 enjoyed a business dinner that ran almost to their closing time, and they did a great job of allowing us to feel unrushed in our own corner of the restaurant.

Views are STUNNING! The restaurant, itself, could use a bit of freshening/updating. Food was good - not spectacular, but not bad either. We ordered plenty of cocktails, appetizers, and main courses, and I didn't hear any complaints.

Great place for a large group to enjoy the best views in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: One of my favorite restaurant in downtown Santa Barbara. Absolutely decent food and service. I ll happy to recommend Opal to all my friends.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: This is the highest rated Italian place in Santa Barbara and that comes as a huge surprise after visiting. 

The service was good, but the food was awful. We went from burnt bread to an extremely bland gnocchi with tomato sauce, and a bow tie pesto that was swimming in olive oil.

I don't recommend unless you're into that sort of thing.

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Fabulous location across from the pier! I loved the ocean views. It was overcast and chilly when I was there so I had to sit inside. I loved the Manhattan clam chowder and the scallops. Great service! I will be back!
Categories: Pizza, Nightlife, Italian, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Excellent pizzas in a cozy atmosphere. Perfect thin crust and fresh ingredients. Solid drink list and an attentive staff.

Categories: Sports Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Food, Barbeque, American (Traditional) | Review: Had their catering and the chicken wings had feathers all over. Otherwise the service was great food was OK.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: One of my favorite spots in Santa Barbara. Great patio, music and prompt service! If there is a line, wait, it's worth it :)

Categories: Food Trucks, Tacos, Mexican, Salad, Food, Restaurants | Review: Very good tacos. Had the carne asada.  The meat was a little crunchy I like that. Never had them like that before.  The tortillas were good not greasy at all. I would come back for sure.

Categories: Food, Live/Raw Food, Restaurants, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food Delivery Services | Review: So fresh and so clean (like the OutKast song). Isn't that what you want out of a place that handles raw fish?

I came in here for ice cream, believe it or not. There's only two places in the world I've ever had Black Sesame, this place and a gelato shop near the river in Florence, Italy. But this place uses the rolling technique you've probably seen in videos of Southeast Asia. 

So the ice cream was insanely good. My fiance chose nutella with fresh bananas (I ended up finishing hers too). 

As I walked out the store, I told the owner (who is super nice and patient) that I would be back for poke. 

Well here I am. Day 2 eating salmon poke with sushi rice and veggies and a sauce he makes personally.  There's a lot of places to eat in SB, but this has quickly become one of my favorites.

Categories: Food, Desserts, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: This was my first time eating nitrogen ice cream and I was really impressed. The staff was very friendly and we got to watch them pour the nitrogen into all of the mixers which makes a fun visual (added about a 10-15 minute wait, but not a big deal). 
I thought the ice cream itself was really rich and creamy. I ordered the Signature Premium Sea Salt Caramel, however I didn't taste anything other than plain vanilla ice cream. Fortunately, I love vanilla so I still enjoyed it, but I'm not sure if they got my order wrong or if the flavors are just seriously lacking taste.
Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: The ambiance was very nice, the waiters were receptive .I would recommend the crabcakes appetizers. The garlic bread was a bit bland though.

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Food, Vegan, Cafes, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Gluten-Free, Vegetarian, Acai Bowls | Review: Overall, a relaxed vegetarian/vegan cafe with a very cute and trendy aesthetic, however my friends and I were left disappointed with our meals. The main reason being PORTION SIZE. We ordered the raw veggie wrap ($10) and the cauliflower crust sandwich ($11) and were nowhere near full after eating. We were also bummed to realize that all our dishes appeared to have significantly less ingredients in them compared to other photos on yelp. I understand that organic, quality ingredients cost more, but there's definitely a point where it just feels like a rip-off, and that is what we experienced today. It's a great concept and it seems like their strongest asset may be their drink selection, but there's improvements that should definitely be made if they want to grow. Until then I'd recommend checking out the Honey B if you're looking for a good, filling vegan spot.

Categories: Specialty Food, Butcher, Meat Shops, Food | Review: This place truly does have the best tri tip in town! 

This place has such a hipster atmosphere and located in the FunkZone close to all kinds of top notch breweries/wine bars. The patio is beautiful and the quality meat here is fresh and flavorful. When you order a steak here they actually do their due diligence and take out a couple cuts to explain to you what each of them would taste like to make sure you get the most personalized meal possible. 

The beer selection on tap was impeccable and it also doubles as a butcher shop for your meat needs on the go. Their prime rib roasts were a little on the expensive side but totally worth it. If you have an Axxess card they have a sweet 2 for 1 deal for trip too which is awesome. 

Shalhoob is solid addition to the FunkZone scene that all yelpers need to check out!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: OMG the best noodles on the planet
People are addicted to this place 
Great spices and wonderful artistic presentation 
Wow wow wow 
Northern Thailand Curry Noodle  " Kho
Sol"

Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: This is a very cozy place with very good food. To start we had chips with two excellent salsas. One was mild and full flavored and another much hotter with the same depth of flavor.  My main course was grilled shrimp in a Diablo sauce with rice and beans, probably the best Mexican preparation of I have had. I think the secret to good Mexican food is great sauces and I think they have that down. I almost forgot the margaritas, they are great, not sweet and syrupy but fresh and clean.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: WOW amazing ambiance and tasty fare!
I've heard of this place so when I was in town I jumped at the opportunity to come. Reservations were easy online, and we lucked out with a table outside by one of the fires. :-) 
Per Yelp recos, we ordered the brussies, the wedge salad, and the scallops. We added the cheeseboard (it was okay but the cheese was kind of sparse. Amazing homemade baguette though!). THE SCALLOPS WERE MY FAV!!!!! Has a small kick and the roasted parsnips and fried tendrils were so delicious =P
After dinner we had a Off the Alpes drink and enjoyed it by the fire.
Next time I want to try to get a table in the booths inside! 
Highly recommend!

Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: I had a "create your own" noodle bowl with meat and veggies.  I also had the popcorn chicken and the pork belly bao.  The food was great.  It was nice to eat food that was tasty and healthy!  The service was also fast even though they were busy.   I will definitely go back for more!

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: I've gotten to try Loquita twice - learned to make an earlier reservation the second time, instead of snagging a last-minute 9pm (although I will say it's still poppin' even then)! 

Bocaditos I've tried:
- El Bulli: a bubble of tomato gazpacho soup - more of a sensory than culinary experience.. a bit like a big savory popping boba. 
- Croqueta de jamon/queso: both super adorable/beautifully plated, perfect crisp breading, great flavor inside. 
- Pan con tomate: like a light crispy flatbread with fluffy tomato on top. 

Tapas:
- Patatas bravas: a huge pile of small potatoes smothered in delicious sauce (a nice complement to the delicateness of everything else)
- Hamachi crudo: bright and fresh. Besides the actual fish, the most randomly intriguing element I remember was the leek ash (it added a subtle smoky flavor)! 

The star of the show, hands down, is the paella (I would die for the paella). I've tried the mariscos which is a treasure chest of seafood as well as the mar y tierra which included huge prawns and chunks of the most tender and juicy short rib. Both paellas are hefty and savory but with fresh creamy and bright acidic elements to round everything out. And the crispy, slightly charred layer on the bottom is the best part (just make sure to get a picture before the server scrapes it for you)! 

The churros, which came with three dipping sauces (chocolate, dulce de leche, and raspberry), were the perfect way to end the meal.

Categories: Soup, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Italian | Review: Good Italian food right here! I have eaten here many times and it is really good. The servers were really nice each time and the portions are decent.

Categories: Steakhouses, American (Traditional), Desserts, Food, Restaurants | Review: From the warm greeting when you enter the door to the sweet waitress that lists the choice of pie for the evening....YOU COULDN'T BE HAPPIER WHEN YOU'RE AT CLEMENTINES! 

I personally love the filet mignon, prime rib, or the fried chicken dinner...


They have these awesome "ranch" bake potatoes.. (fried I think?) 

The salad dressings are YUM!


The pie is AMAZING. 


All homemade and a variety of flavors. (LOVE THE BANANA CREAM AND COCONUT CREAM PIE... SO GOOD!)


Best pie HANDS DOWN. 


Service is great you feel right at home!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants | Review: Awesome spot to grab a bite to eat. This place is legit for the mere fact that it is very unassuming. We chose this spot because it was a market/restaurant. From my experience fish is best cooked and prepared by those who are in the market of selling it. I ordered the swordfish along with a pint of Central Calfornia's Finest Firestone Ale.   
I would recommend this to anyone who wants to relax and indulge in great seafood.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: My bf and I loved our meal so much, including the great service. The seafood paella was the best part of the meal. The octopus was also outstanding.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Caterers, Mexican, Salad, Event Planning & Services, Desserts, Food, Nightlife | Review: I think this place is honestly Yelp overrated and hyped up

Came to try it out on a few occasions based on good reviews from coworkers & after reading some yelp reviews, and have tried the burritos and Maria's enchiladas. The enchilada sauce was whatevs. Something crucial seemed to have been missing from both of these dishes for me that wasn't cutting it for me or making me want to run back tomorrow to get something else. They were on the bland side, and the best part about this place has actually been the salsa bar (which made the dishes MUCH better) and warm chips that i've had to request on the side (for free at least), but then again, many places have an assorted salsa bar

BOTH were mediocre and i've tried a solid amount of Mexican food in my life, it nothing dazzling or special enough to make me want to come back or make me dying to instagram a foodie pic and rave about it, and i'm a bit disappointed because I thought I could trust some of these reviews!! Lets just say I could be as easily pleasured and satisfied from something from Chevys. & If this place and Los Agaves were to have a battle, i'd choose Los Agaves in a heartbeat

The pricetag is also what kills this place for me, the enchiladas and burritos were pretty small (12+ dollars for 2 s/m sized enchiladas). Overall, not impressed, what would only keep me coming back are the delish & solid salsas

Although I have heard good things about the Pozole (perhaps one more lucky chance)

Sometimes some of the workers give off a strange and unfriendly vibe as well that makes me want to dip as soon as im done! Some type of scheming or lightweight secretive haterade that radiates through. Some fans or AC would be nice also as I was sizzling in there from the summer/fall heat!

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: We tried to get a reservation for three of us on a Saturday night about a week out. The earliest we could reserve a table was 9:30 pm...kinda late for us to have a big dinner, but it made us feel very European. The place was packed, and we didn't get seated until about 9:40 pm. Our waitress did a fine job explaining the menu, and she made some good recommendations. Overall the service was very good - from hostess/hosts, managers, waitress, servers, bus boy - all very attentive and kind at the end of another busy Saturday night. We started with the brussel sprouts, deviled eggs, and oysters on the half shell. The brussel sprouts were fantastic, and my son loved the deviled eggs. The oysters were just ok...nothing special, and the worst thing we ate all night. We continued with the beet salad and the acorn squash salad. Both were delicious! My wife and son shared the steak, and they loved it - perfectly seasoned and cooked. I had the fish special which was the neck of a fish. It was amazing...one of the best fish experiences I have ever had. We had no room for dessert, but they sounded good. The chef here is extremely creative and the service is great! The place is just too popular...plan well ahead for reservations!

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: My husband & i came in on a Thursday around 1:30pm. It was dead except for maybe 5 people. So, that may be the reason i rate this place so well. 

Our server Chelsea was very attentive and met all of our needs; the drinks were good and the food was delicious. We missed happy hour, but it seemed like they had good deals going on later. 

The only negative part didn't even happen to us. There was either a drunken or mentally disabled man (I'm sorry, i honestly couldn't tell the difference based on the few minutes i witnessed him) who came into the bar--the staff seemed to know him. He kept trying to talk to a woman sitting by herself & she was just NOT having it. He repeatedly tried to talk to her, and she pretty much stared down at her phone and food, completely ignoring him. It would have been nice if the staff tried to politely steer him on his merry way (& he wasn't partaking in any food or beverage there), since they knew him. I only say that because it was absolutely cringe-worthy and kind of sad to see this woman convey such discomfort at this man talking to her (& the staff saw it). Since she didn't say anything, who cares, i guess? But for her sake, if he was asked to come back later or something, maybe it wouldn't have seemed so bad instead of us all just watching it happen.

Categories: Restaurants, Mongolian, Chinese | Review: I have only tried the mongolian grill and buffet, so I can't attest to the quality or taste of the menu items. The mongolian grill and buffet, however, are amazing. The ingredients for the Mongolian grill are always fresh and there is a great assortment of them, including a wide variety of sauces and oils to season your concoction with. The grill chef is always friendly and efficient as well. The buffet always has something good to offer, too (I prefer the cream cheese wontons and peppered eggplant). I have heard that this place gets pretty crowded during lunch, but I can attest to the fact that there is not much of a crowd there most evenings, and they do the buffet and Mongolian grill every night until 8:30.

One thing to note: if you are over 5'6", do not sit at a booth. For some reason, the booth tables are really low. I am 6' tall, and the table comes up to about my belly button.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Food was cold and tastes like it has been made hours ago. Had high hopes but probably won't be visiting again :/

Categories: Food, American (New), Thai, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Desserts, Cafes, Sandwiches, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: Wow! What a neat little place. Great beer and great food! Bahn Mi sandwich was amazing! Great fusion eatery with fun atmosphere.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Stopped here for lunch on a Thursday afternoon and had a great lunch. We had fish tacos and steamed clams and both were excellent! The indoor seating and view of the beach cannot be beat. There is free parking and the restaurant is right on the beach. We will be back with our beach chairs to enjoy the beach before or after our next meal. We can't wait to return to this scenic location.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Burgers, American (New) | Review: Extremely average. The onion rings had no taste to them and required a ton of sauce to make them appealing, however the BBQ and ranch couldn't beat McDonalds.

Categories: Soup, Thai, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: DO NOT ORDER TO GO. Honestly, this is too expensive for the soup. It's $8.65 for a bowl which I guess it's fine for dining in. However, I ordered it to go and they gave me such a small portion!!! The bowl in person is so much bigger when you dine in. Save yourself from a rip off. I poured the whole soup in a bowl that was similar to size of the one in person and it didn't even fill it up all the way. The chicken in it was also pretty chewy which is gross. I posted photos of the soup dine in and to go (using my blue bowl at home) for comparison
.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Chinese, Restaurants | Review: BUFFET Review ONLY

Ok, so let's start with saying that this is a very affordable deal- IF you can find something on the buffet that you like.

I'd like to recommend the following off the buffet;

Wonton Soup- I like this sometimes, when it's got some flavor to it and there is a nice coat of grease on top. Sometimes, this soup is completely tasteless and too adpted to a low- calorie diet..The "dumplings" in the soup are very smooth but I usually don't like the filling, which tastes like bacon that's gone bad.

Chicken Curry- last time I visited, this wasn't even served, which was a huge disappointment. When it is served, unless it's too spicy, it's very good and will also soften the white rice, if it's sat in the pan too long and gone hard and/ or dry.

Chicken wings- These are aslo really good, if they haven't dried out in the pan.

Beef Broccoli- Is usually very good and, in 99% of instances, the broccoli is neither over- or under cooked.

Garlic String Beans- Rarely fail. These are really good!

Jell-O for dessert- has a strange but good flavor so give that a try.

The servers have always been very friendly and prompt when I've visited so I have absolutely no complaints there. Nor do I have any trouble with the price, obviously, IF I can eat at least a few things off the buffet.

My coclusion is that it's a gamble coming here for the buffet, and you should definitely check it out before being seated. If items look dry and or are missing- go someplace else.

A++

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: This is my favorite ice cream place, it's better than Häagen Dazs or Ben and Jerry's and that's really saying something. Their ice cream has an amazingly texture and they've got some really unique flavors that are all over the top delicious. Peppermint is probably my favorite. I've only tried the ice cream but I'd imagine their frozen yogurt is equally good.  The only knock I've got against it is that it's pretty expensive for ice cream, but certainly worth it every once in a while. 

I read that they are opening up two expansion locations in Los Angeles 2014, which is great news because that's where I live. 

Cheers!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Located inside the public market near the back. Seems like a popular stall since the public market is small and not a lot of stalls are open.  

Got the Wednesday special: pad see ew! This is my go-to order for thai food. The texture of the noodles,  the chicken, and the vegetables were all on point, but the flavor was lacking.  Usually pad see ew is a lot darker too, from the soy sauce, but this was very light in color.  There was some pepper sprinkled in the top and that helped add some flavor but still missing that original sweet soy sauce flavor.

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: At the last minute Oliver's cancelled our reservation due to a "rain storm". I've heard of their great reviews and I'm highly disappointed in their customer service. The manager "Phyllis" told us the kitchen closed at 8:30PM 20 minutes before our reservation... Highly disappointed. Take you're chances hunties!

Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque | Review: Woodys in Santa Barbara has always been a local staple and for a good reason.  They have always been the place in town to go to for good BBQ.  This is the kind of place that I honestly hope never goes away - I have been going to woodys since I was a kid.  It's just a great local BBQ joint. 
      The idea that this is a great spot was reinforced last night when I had the Mother Clucker.  Onion rings on ANY sandwich is always a good sign..... and so freakin good. 
    If you have never been to this place - you NEED to check it out.

Categories: Food, Custom Cakes, Bakeries, Patisserie/Cake Shop | Review: I was parked in the bakery parking lot after purchasing many conchas and some lunch.
The owner manager actually came out and yelled at me about being parked in his parking lot not knowing I had just made a purchase.

This used to be a family tradition and our company would always order from here. No more. This treatment was traumatic....

Panaderia Verinica is far more authentic and welcoming.

Bottom line owner/manager ruined it. We grew up with this place and he had the audacity to treat a patron like a tresspasser.

Categories: Nightlife, American (Traditional), Cafes, Restaurants, Wine Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, Bars | Review: The food is great. The people are great. And the atmosphere is chill.  The bar is relatively small but theres 2. Both are good to hang out and watch some sports. Esuan is the bartender. Hes fast and cool as hell.

Categories: Food, Desserts, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: I went here in the middle of the day, and there was almost no line! I ordered an ice cream sandwich, and a normal scoop. Although the prices are expensive, the taste and quality of each flavor is very good. However, they had given us the wrong flavor of ice cream for the sandwich. I did not realize it at the time, however after a couple minutes of eating it something didnt taste right. The employees did not attempt to find us to correct this mistake. Other than that everything was very good.

Categories: Active Life, Bowling, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Party & Event Planning, Arcades, Event Planning & Services, Arts & Entertainment | Review: Great bowling, great bar, staff is okay.

For something to do outside the bars in SB- GO TO ZODOS. Wed night= cheap beer/college night.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Weird place, which is a shame, as it's in a great location. Doesn't seem to know whether it's a dive bar, or a down-at-heel cafeteria. The drunks at the bar think the former, the disappointed diners the latter. SB has enough restaurants - if this place went full-Dive bar (with a slight refurb), it'd be a

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Get The tugboat (eggs Benedict ) because the hollandaise is yummy and the eggs perfectly cooked and best ham too .

Sweet cakes was that sweet.

Like the fact it's a casual Brekkie place and filled with beautiful people.

Categories: Grocery, Specialty Food, Food, Health Markets, Local Services, Community Service/Non-Profit | Review: I love this place so much! So much so that I wanted to work there. Didn't get the job because they weren't hiring, but hey, it's chill. I turned vegan a little over 3 months ago and this place is my haven. Especially living in FT and having the crappy Portola food. Sometimes, literally the only thing i could eat was cereal. So I'd get on my bike, ride out to the co op, pick up a BLT sandwich (WHICH IS DELICIOUS) and sit there and enjoy the food I was eating, for once. The wall of self serve nuts, granola, etc is great and the vegan chocolate selection is amazing. I'm so appreciative of this place. I love the vibe too. There's one guy that works there that isn't my favorite, but i think it's because he's british and british people tend to be kinda rude. There's one girl that works there and she's too sweet! I remember I went in for my interview and she was so friendly, calling me sweetie and letting me know I could sit down at the reserved table to wait for Mo. Sidenote: their cucumber water is the bomb diggz. I've never actually bought it, but I sampled it from that girl and it's so good. Anyway, any time I've ever tried their fruit, it's always been nice and sweet and ripe and delicious. As I mentioned, I lived in FT and had a meal plan, so I didn't have to buy my own food a lot, but I'm going into my second year living on Madrid with no meal plan, so I'm sure I'll be at the co-op A LOT. Doesn't hurt that my room mate has been vegan her whole life and can show me the secret vegan treasures of the co-op. Also, I love that the products are locally grown/produced because it has less of an impact on the environment and help those small town farmers against the corporate monsters of Monsanto. The extra money spent on the organic produce is worth it when you think of all the chemicals and pesticides that infect non organic foods. But if you become a member and/or volunteer there at least 4 hours a month, you get a discount, so i think it's well worth it. The membership is only $30 for the whole year and I just got added as a secondary onto my room mate's membership, so i get all the benefits for only $15 a year :) I suggest taking that route. Or paying the $100 for the lifetime membership. Even if you're only staying in IV for for years, the $100 is less than $120. But again, if you do the same thing I do and split it with a room mate, you're better off not getting the lifetime membership. ANYWAY, this place is great and I've really fallen in love with it! Shop here!

Categories: Japanese, Food, Live/Raw Food, Poke, Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: I had a GREAT lunch here.  It was beautifully prepared and delicious and the service was just great. And it's gorgeous to boot. I'm going to be coming here a lot.  Loved my poke bowl!!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: We were visiting Santa Barbara and I wanted some good drinks. I got the mango and my husband got the Paloma margarita. Soooo delicious the bartender knows how to mix. The food was so good and reasonably priced for what your gonna get. The pastor tacos where the best I ever had. The green avacado salsa was delicious. Totally worth the stop. You won't regret it.

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Nightlife, Karaoke, Asian Fusion, Restaurants | Review: Only been to China once but this place tasted legit.    The spicy fish fillet is the bomb, staff are friendly and attentive.   

Patronize this place!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: I hate giving crappy reviews, but hey... they started it with the crappy food!
My food was cold, salty and tasted almost like the ingredients were frozen. Service was sub-par. 
Come on Casa Blanca, if you want to play with the big boys in Santa Barbara you are going to at least have to give a little effort!

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: The food was delicious. We went at sunset and the only complaint I have is the sun burning my eyes out! Where they sat us was so uncomfortable. I feel for the upscale restaurant it is that should had not of Been something that was so difficult to fix. The prices are very high for the portions. The food is wonderful and the quality is great. Either expect to eat after or spend upwards of 200+ for 4 people

Categories: Bakeries, Food Trucks, Food, Cupcakes | Review: My nieces and nephew freaked out at the cookie dough balls covered in chocolate.  What a cute presentation too (tucked into an egg carton!  Delicious and oh, so chic!

Categories: Nightlife, Restaurants, Steakhouses, Bars | Review: After having a piss poor experience at Ahi Sushi (we up and left after waiting nearly an hour for our food), my wife, children, and I went next door to Tee-Off to try our luck there (for further on Ahi Sushi, see Ahi Sushi review).  Before entering, however, I asked two patrons who had just come out of the restaurant if it was still open.  They enthusiastically stated it was and told us it was a great place to eat!  

We went inside and we were promptly greeted and seated.  Service was swift, and we received fresh vegetables, hot bread, soups, salads, then our main entrees (in quick succession and in that order).  Our fussy 3 year old was appeased very quickly, to our delight, and his whiny complaints of hunger ceased!  I told our waitress, Nicole, about our experience next door at Ahi Sushi, and she was very sympathetic and caring.  When I told her they even charged me $2.16 for my can of Coke on the way out, which I still had with me (there was no way I was going to leave Ahi Sushi without that can after paying $2.16 for it), SHE APOLOGIZED and offered me Coke on the house!!!  I was like, "Really?!"  She took that God forsaken lukewarm can of Coke from me, along with the nightmarish memory, and replaced it with a fresh tall glass of ice cold Coke.  Wow.

My wife got the half chicken with garlic mashed potatoes and vegetables for her two sides, and I ordered the prime rib (which is what they are known for) with vegetables and onion rings for my two sides.  Both the chicken and prime rib were outstanding.  The chicken was soft, juicy, and tender, and the prime rib was delicious as well.  The onion rings were outstanding, too.  Nicole told me they make the onion rings in-house!  The food, along with Nicole's outstanding service, turned our night around for the better!  Overall, Tee-Off offered us an outstanding dining experience!

Now... I have to say something about our waitress, Nicole.  She was one of THE friendliest, pleasant, and personable servers I've had the pleasure of having in a loooong time.  She was all smiles and so good spirited the entire time we were there.  She even went out of the way to amuse our little ones.  I can't say enough about Nicole and the level of service she provided.  She single-handedly turned a bad night in to a great night!  Thanks Nicole!

Categories: Moroccan, Restaurants | Review: Chef Karim is a sweetheart!  He came out many times to chat with us and other patrons and really made us feel at home.  The proportions are generous and the waitstaff is very friendly.

My friend and I placed our orders and minutes later Chef Karim came out and told us we had both ordered sweeter dishes.  He wanted us to know in case we wanted to change our order to something spicier.  So we changed one of our selections and he brought out a sample of the other dish for us to taste for free anyway!  The food was pretty tasty. 

I'm not a fan of belly-dancers but the one at Chef Karim's was really friendly and fun, and not a cheap imitation like belly-dancers at lame theme restaurants tend to be!

Categories: Restaurants, Cafes, Food, American (New), Diners | Review: I wanted to treat my 15 year old son to lunch while we were downtown between appointments and go someplace a little nicer for the occasion so we went to the Nordstrom Cafe. Sadly, not one dish is sustainable, aka plant based. Every single dish has some animal flesh or secretion included. I asked the chef who happened to be by the register and he said I could take the chicken off a salad for example or the cheese, but then that becomes an expensive head of lettuce. No thanks. I guess Nordstrom does not want vegans shopping the Anniversary Sale. That's fine, we took our dollars elsewhere.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: We had an early Friday night dinner at the Lark as we couldn't get reservations for later times, despite booking a week ahead.  Probably one of the trendiest places with decent food in the area hence all the hype.  Our server was fantastic - he was warm, friendly and highly knowledgeable of the menu.  As the night progressed, they took little longer and did forget some of my requests.  Overall, good food and fantastic service.  Nice date night venue.

Categories: Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Gluten-Free, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: Great place - coffee is delicious and low/no sugar is the default.  Muffins and carrot cake also fantastic - low sugar, GF etc.  Well worth checking out!
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Fresh chips, tasty salsa choices.. the chili replenish are my favorite with an order of chips and salsa!!!

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: Great location and nice outdoor seating. Hillstone food has always been delicious and consistent. I get that they need to turn tables in under an hour but today the rush-rush was ridiculous. Every five minutes there was a checkpoint. Am I allowed to put my fork down for a minute to have a conversation? The second we both paused our server wanted to whisk our plates away. Then the drinks got clear before we finished and we asked to keep them...so the glasses were slammed back on our table and server stormed off. There was not a lineup waiting for seating. Plenty of other options next time.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: It was very good! Very pricey though. Very crowded though, so make sure to make reservations an hour in advance.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Desserts | Review: I've had Ali's cupcakes and I know for a fact they are a 5-star experience all the way. She bakes them perfectly and has the most outstanding flavors that you literally could not find anywhere else in the world. Not only does she bake killer cupcakes but she designs the greatest cupcake wrappers as well! 

Did I mention she won CUPCAKE WARS on the Food Network???!! I mean, really, there is no competition out there for this girl. Get her for your wedding! Get her for your party. Get her before it's too late and she is only making cupcakes as private baker to Angelina and Brad.

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: This is hands down our favorite place to eat! Oliver's offers drinks, starters, entrées and desserts all plant-based using ingredients I would use at home but with a creative twist summing up an ultimate party in my mouth. The kids love it too, tipping the review to a full 5! Oliver's has perfected fine-dining for vegan couples and families. Can't wait to share our experience with our friends! BTW, the service is impeccable, as is the establishment. (And be sure to check out the extra touch in the gorgeous rr - Jo Malone hand soap! Love it!)
My favorites: cocktails, zucchini flatbread, mushroom tostada, pad thai, & chocolate tort. Servings are large and delicious!

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Wow this place is the real deal!! We were able to sit on the patio overlooking the ocean which I highly recommend! The service was great, the food was delicious, and the atmosphere was also a plus. 

We ordered a lot of food and drinks to try almost everything! Here's what I would recommend:
- mussels with pork belly (the pork belly was sooo tender!)
- tiger prawns ( the sauce was delicious but you only get three shrimps)
- the lobster Mac and cheese - so creamy 
- the scallops with bacon jam (very tasty but the scallops were a bit chewy)

Just alright
- fisherman's platter (it's tasty and a lot of seafood but it wasn't anything spectacular!)

Not recommend:
- the striped sea bass with wild trice (too bland and did not have a tonic flavor)

Also don't forget the lava cake at the end!!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: A little pricey for the food. But it was yummy and the specialty drinks are amazing, and strong! There are beautiful views and it's a good atmosphere. We really enjoyed our dinner here. It has been my favorite so far in Santa Bárbara!

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: I was at this restaurant yesterday...and I have to say that my entire family was disgusted of their food! We were four people and not one of us took a second bite of their food!! I am extremely shocked that this place has 3 stars let alone has people giving them 5 stars! It doesn't even deserve one star! If I could I wouldn't even give them one. Will not be returning and will be warning friends and families not to come here as well. Overall, horrible experience first time we left a restaurant hungry!
Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood, Bars | Review: Service was horrible. The server did her best, but it was obvious the management does not know how to schedule..

Categories: Restaurants, Tacos, Mexican | Review: My tacos were great! Especially the salsa verde tacos. But, the first thing I did notice while ordering my food was the dirty floor and the filthy bathroom! I was a bit worried about my food while waiting. I'll give this place a

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: LOVE the Monte Cristo and service is fast and friendly. When they were still doing dine-in, our two dishes of food were ready in less than 10 min. Monte Cristo is honestly enough for two people to be pleasantly full if you get the side of hashbrowns. Staff is friendly and hard working, prices are very fair.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: A local review for locals:
Definitely a tourist trap now. Maybe lunch would be ok but dinner is way too expensive. The main menu items are in the mid $30's. ONE crab cake appetizer: $12. One drink: $12. This is definitely hotel high-end prices. Our local big hotels, Tee-Off, Harry's, Boathouse are in this price range but you get a lot more (food/view/service). Let's face what cajun food really is- poor peoples food. Catfish is one of the cheapest fish you can buy, rice stew, etc, etc. There is a menu page of pasta that gets the price down to $25. But three of us went there for dinner, ordered two pasta dishes and blackened catfish (with two or three rose potatoes and some sauteed zucchini which is what we make at home many nights- nothing special or hard to do and frankly we do it better), no apps, one martini to share, one bread pudding to share (that wasn't ready on time even though we ordered it on time with our meal) and voilà: $95.$15 tip and we're at $110. Really? For catfish and pasta? I'm never going back. PS my last grocery trip on Sunday for the whole week was $110 so as a percentage of food budget, this was a huge expense.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, French, Vegan, Seafood, Asian Fusion, Gluten-Free, American (New) | Review: This is a very cozy and quaint restaurant run by very food conscious people. The menu is written for people with food sensitivities (gluten free, soy free, etc) and eating lifestyles (vegan, vegetarian). We had the curry soup and the miso soup, both excellent and for dinner we had the Greek moussaka and the stuffed salmon. The ingredients were amazingly fresh.  I highly recommend this cozy little restaurant.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Upon arriving we were told to sit wherever we would like.  Since it was a nice day out we sat in one of two patio tables they have.  

I ordered the bacon Gouda scramble with a biscuit and potatoes.  The potatoes were soft and flavorless. Everything else was ok. 

Overall, would not recommend.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Vegan, Delis, Gluten-Free | Review: My boyfriend and I were visiting santa babara this weekend and we're both vegans so of course we came here due to all the vegan options! 
The employee helping us, Alex, was incredibly knowledgable despite not being vegan himself. Once he found out we were vegan he even checked the chips we'd chosen and made sure the complimentary suckers he gave us were vegan. We go to santa barbara a few times a year and we will certainly be coming here every time we visit! 
My boyfriend also runs a vegan food instagram account with 10k+ followers and will be posting a more detailed review there in a few days(: @laveganfoodshare 

Thanks again Alex! You brightened our day!

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: enjoyable atmosphere, good service from the hostess to the valet.  Food was good, but not amazing.  the drinks were really good.  two of the dishes were ok, one very good (roast chicken).  Gnocchi was really over salted.  Crudo, vegetables were cut really large and didn't seem like other crudos I've had.  A bit expensive for the food.  Would be nice if there was a place to sit or before they call your reservation.  The bar was totally full so had to stand in the walkway until our table was ready.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: I fully admit I'm biased. Gluten Free sandwiches are becoming more common but they're still not exactly ubiquitous. Pickles & Swiss is very easy going about making it Gluten Free and distinguishes between a preference for Gluten Free bread and needing your sandwich to be Gluten contamination free, where they assemble it on a different surface with fresh protective gear and washed knives and the whole deal. It's deeply appreciated. And, the sandwiches are good, which more often than not you're trading off when you're looking out for your health. It IS gluten free bread so they're stuck with those limitations but it's at the Udi level quality, where it tastes ok and not like shredded cardboard to hold fixing until it gets to your mouth. So, that's also a huge plus. The innards of the sandwiches are high quality and make you feel like you're actually getting something worth paying for, rather than just you don't have the time and energy to do it so you're getting someone else to make the sandwich for you. 

My personal favorite is the Norm's Cold Cut. It comes a bit smaller than usual because of the Gluten Free Bread but the taste is worth it. 

I also feel like I should note that the owner is very involved. I've seen him at both locations. I've seen him actually working as a sandwich preparer at the Goleta location. He notices people who show up. He notices what we're ordering. And he even stopped by my table once when I was at the Paseo Nuevo location to talk to me about the differences in the locations. Which all just gives the impression of someone who is actually really interested in customer opinion in a deep way. It makes it one of those places where I just am not worried about a drop in quality because he's just too into it. He's committed in a way I've very rarely seen. Which is very reassuring for the quality of it all.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Went here for dinner twice.  We like it.  It does get loud, so we have enjoyed sitting near the edges of the restaurants (not right in the middle) because the front and back tables seems to be a little easier to hold conversation.  

Food was delicious.  Have had prime rib ravioli, Caesar salad, asparagus and prosciutto, gnocchi, veal scappolini, fish special, and a meat special.  Everything is really yummy.  Portions are nice sized- salads enough to share and appetizers are also enough to share.

Service was great.  Friendly, helpful, humor, and attentive.

We came at 7.45 on a Saturday and there were no empty tables, so it was a good thing we had a reservation.  Previously we came at 6.30 on a Saturday and also had made a reservation.  During the dinner hour, every table seems to be full, which is always a good sign!

Love love love the tiramisu!

Categories: Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: Had a nice lunch very cool place. Excellent the core little bit minimalist but with a little bit of flair. Lots of wine decent bar really tasty drinks I had some sort of Russian thing very nice. The waiter manager/owner little bit and friendly but maybe he was busy. I had some BLTs salmon sandwich that was really good enjoyed it great location maybe a little bit more friendly on waiter/manager side. .

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Bottle service for my sisters bachlorette party. Amazing venue but the service fort the venue wasn't up to par, no one checked on us or gave us mixer refills unless we tracked our waitress down. Overall m cool and beautiful place especially with 3 dance floors but service needs improvement.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Desserts, Wine Bars, Bars, Italian, Nightlife | Review: I was so excited to try out the food here as Oprah said it was wonderful. Especially the meatballs. I ate half a meatball. It was bland and I didn't realize it contained raisins yuk. 
My friends pizza was nice. I liked my vegans dessert. But have no desire to return. 
Service friendly and very nice.

Categories: Bars, Mediterranean, Restaurants, Nightlife, Moroccan, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Italian | Review: We loved the location right by the ocean, loved the outdoor seating, and loved the service! The only thing that was slightly less than ideal was the variety of menu options, but we still found something we absolutely loved! It's a very romantic and relaxing venue -- would definitely recommend for a date night!

Categories: Lounges, American (New), Burgers, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Sports Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: This place is great and a fun find, HOWEVER the male bartenders are so rude. We have been here 2 nights in a row because we like the atmosphere and it's fun. BUT both nights the bartenders were so damn rude and didn't seem like they even wanted to serve us or work there! 
Great place, WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Tex-Mex, American (Traditional), Burgers, Food, Sandwiches, Mexican, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: This spot has healthy and tasty options, what more could a person possibly ask for. Whenever I go I always get the portabella mushroom sandwich and the when I'm feeling extra I'll add avocado to it as well. 

My friends rant and rave over the Kelly burger and go back almost once a week for it. I haven't had it myself but I would definitely suggest it solely based off the way they promote it. 

Their smoothies are also delicious and aren't filled with syrup like some other places. This past time I got the Aloha Bra which was pretty tasty and not overall sweet.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: The most comforting and uniquely delicious Mexican food around. The drinks are also highly recommended. Must try it if you haven't already.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: We love this place! A tiny hole in the wall, doesn't look like much, but YUM! Staff is not the friendliest, but the food is great and SO reasonably priced (hard to find that combo here in SB!), that the level of service isn't that important.
Definitely stop in here whether you're a local or just passing through. Consistently delicious and you'll be pleasantly surprised at how inexpensive your bill will be!
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Wow wow wow it was delicious quick inexpensive and the quality amazing cannot wait to go back! Taco Tuesday was awesome! I wish you opened in sf!

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: We ordered the chef special which was a noodle with soup and I was floored. I like this so much that I am worried I won't like any other Thai place from now on. Maybe I am exaggerating but it tasted like that. It was so fresh and none of the flavors were overwhelming. And no exaggeration that this was the best mango sticky rice we have ever had. The nice touch of the mint really picks up the senses

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs | Review: We were exhausted and just wanted a place to hang out for a while. Took a Lyft to Dargan's and discovered a gem of a place. Food and drinks were great. Patrick was a bonus!  We felt like we were with an old friend!  Definitely a return visit whenever we are in the area!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (New), French | Review: The entire group i was with really felt this place to be wonderful. Small, family owned high-end place that's not a tourist trap. It was recommended by two locals and they were spot on. 
Liz, chef's wife was warm and welcoming. I let her pick my dinner and the accompanied wine. Great job!  What I greatly appreciated were the prices. I wanted a special meal and time and expected to pay more. Meal+dessert+bottle of wine....150.00. Return?  Heck yeah!

Categories: Thai, Restaurants | Review: Your Place used to be my favorite Thai food in town - I am obsessed with their curries (especially red and green curries) and their chicken Tom Kah. They are my go-to for random nights when I don't want to cook, and obviously whenever I have a Thai craving.

The other night, though, it was $50 for 2 entrees, an appetizer and soup to-go! We are usually able to feed 2 people for dinner and leftover lunch on this amount, but this time the curry was in a container half the size as the usual one. Not only was our meal more expensive, but with the curry being so much smaller, we had way less food than we normally do. I used to be able to do 2 dinners and a lunch from the curry, as well as having extra sauce to make 2 more lunches with my own chicken and veggies. It was the best deal!

While the food is amazing, I probably won't be going to Your Place anymore. If they return to the full-size curry that actually serves 2 people, or lower their prices, then I'll go back to being a loyal customer.

Categories: Chinese, Restaurants | Review: If you are craving for some local Chinese food, u should go to this place. I am a huge fan. Upon entrance, I was warmly greeted. The owner was really polite and makes sure all customers are satisfied. The place was clean and had a nice ambiance to it. 

The griddle potato chips in a hotpot  are amazing. Every single piece is flavorful and crispy. It was so delicious. 

The porkbelly in soy source was not too fat or greasy. It has some snowpea tips underneath which are my favorite vegetable dish here. Sometime people won't expect really fresh vegetable in Chinese restaurant but it surprise me that the vegi here are fresh  and tender. 

Chopped sautéed string bean with minced  meat were my fav dish of the night. I just can't stop eating the chopped string bean. It has jalapeño inside but not too spicy. Flavor in every bite.

Categories: Restaurants, Mongolian, Chinese | Review: SO GOOD!  Is it all about the buffet and menu?  No!  It's all about the Mongolian BBQ.  You walk in, see the chef in his glass case of emotion.  Surrounding his case are delicious goodies, or gifts, for us to make om nomz out of.  You may need to try a few different concoctions before you score a winner and fall in love for yourself.  My bad boy: noodles, beef, broccoli, sprouts, and of course 2 scoops of garlic, 1 scoop of Mongolian BBQ sauce, 1 scoop of chili oil, and 1-2 scoops of chili sauce.  I like my goodies spicy!  

So I have my goods in a bowl, put it on the counter near the window so Mr. Chef can throw the ingredients onto his holy canvas.  He works his magic and voila!  I'm ready to go to town.  

Never have I been disappointed with Mongolian BBQ.  I've tried their buffet items and they are disgusting.  Don't waste your time filling your plate with food that has been sitting under a heat lamp, find true love over on the Mongolian BBQ side.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: One of the first places I tried when I moved to Santa Barbara and I'm so glad that I went here - it really set the bar for everything else.  

This place has delicious drinks and food and the atmosphere and staff really make the experience there memorable.  

I had their cajun martini to start off with and be warned - it has a kick! Strong martini with a strong chili jalepano flavor - it was very good - one of those drinks that you sip throughout dinner.  Luckily we got their margarita as well and it was delicious!  Their drinks were very decently priced and strong - just the way I like them.  

The cajun poppers are delicious!  And their assortment of bread and muffins (there was like 6 different kinds!) that come with it were also very yummy.  

Their crawfish etouffee was great!  And you must end the night off with their souffle - it was delicious!  Everything, from the staff to the food and drinks - was great - I would definitely come back!
