Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: Worst sushi around!! If you like room temperature or even slightly warm sushi, this is your place!!! Yuck....

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: From syart to finish the team at Bouchon delivered.  We were there to celebrate our 36th anniversary, and were seated to a table covered with rose petals, a nice touch!  We started with the scallop appetizer for 2.  3 large sea scallops, each prepared differently, all delicious.  Our entrees were the Lobster Pot Pie, which was off the hook delicious, and was perfect and, the Wagyu Rib Eye, cooked mid-rare and served over spot on vegetables.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Wine Bars | Review: Excellent, inviting atmosphere with a chic, hip style. 

It was a battle getting into the place because a show at the Grenada had just finished; however it was well worth it! I thoroughly enjoyed the atmosphere (minus the overly entangled PDA display of a couple next to the bar) and I loved the clever drink menu and decent selection of wines. The noise level was perfect; I could have a conversation without shouting and straining to hear. 

I will definitely be back! Especially knowing there is a monthly champagne club event. :)

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: Sharkeez never disappoints. The food is great and the frozen Margaritas are my favorite! I have tried a bit of everything from their menu and every time I go I seem to have a new favorite. 

If you are in Santa Barbara I would definitely suggest dropping by for their great food, tasty drinks and fun nightlife! You won't regret it!

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Mediterranean | Review: Extremely disappointing and disgusting experience.

Having brought friends in for the weekend, booked a table outside for 5 for Sunday, September 2nd at 8PM to cap our celebration weekend. We arrived on time at 8 to be told, that they did not have a table outside available for us and did not know when it would be free. 
Unfortunate but things happen. We decided we would wait at the bar, order a cocktail while we wait for the table to be ready. 
That's when things got very strange. The bartender complained to our faces that he can't take any customers at the bar because he is too busy, so in essence, passively telling us we aren't welcome there. 
We brought this up to the hostess and another employee and again asked when our table will be ready. First, they said, the bartender is the only one working tonight and he has a lot going on due to the scheduling, secondly, they said, we don't know when your table will be ready. 
We asked to speak to a manager and were told, there is no manager. 

So, the short story is -
1. Didn't honor a reservation
2. Wouldn't serve us at the bar
3. Apologized for being so busy
4. Apparently doesn't have a manager

We made the drive up to town, we brought guests from overseas and passive attitudes ensured we had a poor experience.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: So tasty, quick, and good bang for your buck. First time having to "make" a poke bowl and the staff was so helpful with giving suggestions. You get so much food (I got a small and was stuffed!) and I was pleasantly surprised by how fresh it all was. Such a great idea for poke bowls!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: This place is perfect for large parties, I had my graduation dinner here. Their happy hour on Monday's is my favorite, they have margaritas that won't disappoint you. I also love how this place has an open patio, pretty much the entire restaurant is open. The staff is always friendly, over all atmosphere is great! The food, the drinks, and the overall location of this restaurant. complementary chips and salsa never disappoint.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Had the other Alambres dish (ranchero) this time and lunch guest had the chicken burrito. 

Both were good but both were too heavy on the salt. 

Free chips were one again appreciated. 

Request for water refill twice seemed to be forgotten.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: First time in Santa Barbara because we were just passing through on our way from OC to the SF bay area and decided to stop off for some brunch. Unfortunately, it was the weekend and everywhere was packed, and as much as we would have liked to sit on the outdoor patio of this quaint little restaurant/bar, we didn't have the time to wait for an outdoor table....and got a little booth inside instead.

So, we went ahead and ordered the French Omelet, Ding's Crispy Chicken Sandwich, and a side of Coleslaw and a Kale salad. Ok, overall, the food was pretty nicely presented, tasted pretty good, but the portion size should be much bigger for what we're paying.

Service was nice, friendly, and attentive enough, and the food pretty decent. I guess it's because this place is located in Santa Barbara that the prices are this high, but I thought the portion sizes for what we paid were a little on the skimpy side. That was fine for us this day as we had a long drive ahead of us and didn't want to get too stuffed anyway.

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Excellent service, beautiful location. Highly recommend the grilled octopus appetizer, it was cooked perfectly.  We got the swordfish, rainbow trout amadine, halibut and panko shrimp. All of us loved what we ordered. It's pricey though, but it's seafood in downtown Santa Barbara.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: I give Boathouse a 5 star for location and view, but really a 1 or 2 star for the food and service. The food was seriously lack-luster. We went for brunch and my husband and I both ordered the chilaquiles, and I got a short stack of pancakes on the side because #pregnancy. The chilaquiles were really quite bland, and I do recognize that I'm from Arizona where the mexican food is REAL mexican food, so I might be a bit spoiled, but it really wasn't good in general.

The pancakes were ok, but were a bit dry. I did, however, eat them all so I probably can't complain too much.

The service was pretty lack-luster. The guy who was supposed to be our server would walk around the tables acting like he was doing something, but then would just walk over to the corner and stand there with a menu in his hand talking to other servers. He made eye contact with us several times before we had even been asked for our drink orders, and it took us literally flagging him down to get him to come over. It also took quite a while for us to get our food - I'm talking like 30-40 minutes. It was a little ridiculous.

The view, though, is second to none. If we ever went back, I'd want to sit at the bar so I can get the full view of the ocean and the dog-friendly beach!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Mediterranean, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Nightlife | Review: Dropped by here on a whim after walking down the wharf and I'm really glad we decided to stop by. Cocktails are absolutely amazing and their bartenders are very knowledgeable and happy to help. We have definitely added a new favorite cocktail to our growing list of cocktails we've discovered while exploring bars - Chareau Me The Money! 

We also tried Figgy Smalls and another drink (completely forgot what it was called), but we loved Chareau so much we had to do another round. 

Amazing interior - very old Hollywood / Hitchcock-esque

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: Another great place for lunch. The meal that I had was fantastic. It has been awhile since I have been there but look forward to another visit.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: There were lots of Mexican Restaurants that Santa Barbara has to offer and this was one of them. We wen to this place to try the Authentic mexican food. It was small but the seating was enough for the customers. We orders tacos (Shrimp and Lengua) and they were awesome! I like how full the taco was and did I mention a lot of Salsas to choose from?! My favorite was the Mango salsa and Roasted Tomato Salsa. Though, I was sort of sad that they didn't give us our free chips and salsa (they actually told us that they will bring it to our table and probably forgot about it). I understand though because it was busy that time we went there. But overall, it was very good for what you pay. I mean $1.50 for a taco?! Like what?! Definitely would recommend you guys to visit this place if you are looking for Authentic and cheap place to eat Mexican food at Santa Barbara.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: Good drinks, friendly staff and a great view! The only reason I'm giving 4/5 stars is because the food is overpriced for what it is.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: The Worker Bee is the best breakfast place within 20 miles! The Worker Benedict is by far my favorite breakfast anywhere. The gravy is fantastic, poached eggs perfect, and the hash browns are the best anywhere. 

We come once a week and have been doing this for over a year. We drive 20 miles also. We have never been disappointed.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: I have frequented the Honor Bar a handful of times and overall have been more than satisfied with my experience. The food and drinks have always been of high quality. However, after my last experience I felt compelled to voice my severe disappointment with the service I received. My server presented herself in a very disingenuous manner. She began well enough giving a friendly greeting. Yet, after asking about the specials she responded and gave a deep eye roll as she walked away. Alaina spent the rest of our time there responding with an air of annoyance whenever we interacted. Since this has been the only time I have had a negative experience I felt that need to share in hopes that this situation can be remedied. Thank you.

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Candy Stores, Chocolatiers & Shops, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Great selection of taffy. My husband was thrilled that they had his Dutch licorice In stock. They also have a good soda selection of no traditional bottled drinks.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: This is my usual stop on my way to Deep Sea Tasting room on the pier.

Their fish is always moist and I enjoy it.  While I like the malt vinegar, I wish they had lemon, or maybe I just need to ask?  Everytime I mention to Grant that I enjoyed, he comes out of the kitchen to appreciate.  I also like his tartar sauce.  Considering the portion/volume, their price is very reasonable.
Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: Delicious deep dish pizza. We nearly didn't come here because some of the reviews we saw weren't great, but I'm glad we did.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Crab Cakes for an Appetizer! Crab Cakes for Main Course! Crab Cakes for dessert! So did I mention the crab cakes? The Palace Grill is great restaurant with amazing food.  It was recommended by our friend and fellow Yelper john n. The ambiance is great but a little crowded inside. You definitely need a reservation or you may end up waiting 40 minutes for a table. They close every day to get ready for dinner so if you get there right when it open at 5;00pm then you be able to sneak in without a reservation.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), French, Restaurants | Review: A lovely restaurant! They were not too busy on a Friday night. My friend was having a wedding nearby and found this restaurant by searching for open reservations for a group of 17, and this was a great choice. We had our own separate dining room so it was great for this rehearsal dinner.

The restaurant is decorated like a lovely house, and there's also a large dining room that would have been the greenhouse, giving diners a view of the surrounding greenery.

If you're looking forward to a view of the lake from the restaurant, unfortunately it's a bit hard to see! There was also some construction material at the edge of the lake blocking our view.

The service was very prompt and professional. 

4 stars:
* Petits Plats - Escargots Persillade ($9.25)
* Plats Principaux - Saumon Oscar (salmon roulade, $33.75)
* Desserts - Warm Rustic Apple Galette ($10.95)

3.5 stars:
* Petits Plats - Artichault Grille (grilled artichoke, $10.50)
3 stars:
* Petits Plats - Tuna Tartare
* Plats Principaux - Coq au Vin ($24.95). No photo. This was packed with flavor, almost a little too much! It was a bit on the sweet side. But the meat was crazy tender & soft.

3 stars:
* Desserts - Flourless Chocolate Cake ($9.95)

2.5 stars:
* Desserts - Creme Brulee (passion fruit. $8.75). No photo. Turns out this was passion fruit-flavored creme brulee, so it was very tarte. Everyone who ordered this was a bit disappointed and couldn't finish this.

See my photos for additional review details!

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Best food ever! I am a picky eater and a vegetarian and it is SO hard to find good vegetarian Thai food even in big cities like la and nyc. The vegetarian pad  was flavorful and had the perfect balance of sweet and spicy. The service and presentation was amazing and it's some of the best Thai food I've ever had!!!

Categories: Food, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis, Grocery | Review: Had the Super Deluxe. First off, it's not Super in taste, size or any other attribute. It is most definitely smaller than a Subway foot-long, has the same kind of meats as the Italian BMT and the quality is no better. It also cost $1.50 more, they offer no options to add on and don't even have spicy mustard. 

The lady who made it wasn't wearing gloves, didn't wash her hands after the previous order and had the voice of a smoker; very appetizing. 

The old woman at the cash register engaged in a 10 minute conversation with the person in front of me during the rush lunch hour. I did not happen to have cash and their credit card minimum is $10 so I had to get out of line and add 2 things I didn't want to my purchase. We live in the 21st century. Places that don't take CC or have minimums are cheap in their very nature. This translates to other aspects of their business. She tried running my card twice and both times it came up cancelled, prompting her to say my CC was cancelled. Idiot. How about you figure out that your machine doesn't take Mastercard and you post a sign stating that fact. Finally, the visa worked. 

Asked for a bag and she threw me the tiniest paper bag in the store (for a sandwich, drink, chips and candy bar) and didn't even offer to bag it until I stared her down.

This place is a joke. I will never go back.

It's not quick. It's not cheap. It's not yummy.

Categories: Specialty Food, Health Markets, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: If I could give Lilac Patisserie 10 stars I would! The sandwich bread was the best I have tasted since going gluten free years ago!!! It was such a treat to eat a yummy sandwich, I have had my fair share of wraps and lettuce wrapped food--but brioche roll?  Almost never!  The bakery items were also delicious, we loved the coffee cake and german chocolate cake, incredible.   The patisserie is gorgeous: clean, inviting and the service was impeccable.  We were out of town visitors but it would be a staple in our house if we were local. Clearly a lot of thought went into all of the details and it shows in every way! We love Lilac Patisserie!

Categories: Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food, Restaurants | Review: The poke here is just not that tasty and is overpriced. It's just moments away from where I live but I refuse to return. I didn't enjoy the taste that much and as much as I love poke I cannot fathom coming back as it's also quite overpriced for the quality.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Latin American, Caterers, Food, Restaurants, Empanadas, Argentine | Review: Such a lovely restaurant and the people working there were so sweet and friendly. My favorite two were the hongos and beef ones! Also loved the outdoor seating area

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Peruvian | Review: Had been to Anchor (prior restaurant) and it was so-so; lots of swagger and inconsistent quality.  Then learned from local vendors about the "attitude" and bad practices of the chef, so was wondering about Blue Tavern. Just had one of the best dinners ever. Ceviche was remarkable. Seafood risotto was killer; branzino was great. Biggest surprise? Cauliflower (my wife's order) was beyond delicious....yes, cauliflower!. Molten chocolate dessert with passion fruit/homemade ice cream was excellent as well. Really different, delicious flavors. Overall, a wonderful dining experience...particularly so given my initial hesitation.

Categories: Restaurants, Korean, Asian Fusion | Review: I loved the rice at Sunny.  It is much healthier and good.  I guess everyone has different taste, which is all good.
If you want authentic Korean food, you have to go to LA but price is reasonable and good food.  You get what you pay for..
Great location too.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: I enjoyed the food here. I felt it was a little expensive, but the flavors were very good and The fish I had tasted fresh. I would come again. The service was also very nice.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: We stopped in for breakfast this morning with our 2 dogs, Zack, Natali and the 2 Sammis made our visit spectacular, they brought our dogs water and 1 sausage each, another group near gave our dogs their leftover pancakes, this place is the best and definitely dog friendly.

Categories: Japanese, Sushi Bars, Restaurants | Review: Fresh sushi, reasonable prices and fantastic friendly service. With just a short walk from the Hampton Inn, I couldn't resist trying this neighborhood dining place out - and I'm glad I did! The wait for my rainbow roll and eel wasn't long at all, but the owner offered me a miso soup while waiting. I declined but sincerely appreciated her offer. Highly recommended!!

Categories: American (Traditional), Seafood, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Fish and chips was awesome! The fish was fresh, flaky, and crunchy! Calamari was not so yummy, was a bit soggy.  The waiters were very nice but very lost ( can't figure out people's food and a milk shake takes 30 mins to make??).

Categories: Bars, Mexican, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: Really disappointed - was expecting so much more after all the good reviews. The food was just not very good. Our family of four agreed that the food was all just flavor-less and meh. I did like the rice and I'm giving 2 stars because the food was packaged well and the to-go blood Orange margarita on the rocks was good.

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: No, no, no. The Hungry Cat doesn't live up to the hype.

Went on a very warm Sunday afternoon a few weekends ago. A friend and I just wanted to have a couple drinks and maybe some appetizers. 

We arrived around 3pm to a nearly empty restaurant (two other small parties dining). After a few minutes of not being greeted (though there were a couple servers walking around near us) my friend asked a server if we could sit at a specific table. The server said "sure" and that she would get us menus and water. Another few minutes passed as we waited for our server, and a short, large woman with bleached short hair came over to our table. I assume she was the manager on duty? She asked us if we could move tables and my friend said "Oh, well, we just asked if we could sit here and the server said we could". The large, bleached hair woman told us the table we were occupying was reserved, and did not apologize for the inconvenience or even smile. So, we moved when we should have left.

The rest of our experience was "eh" as we each had a drink that was pretty good as far as taste, but stingy on the alcohol. The cheese plate was fine, but I could have gone to Trader Joe's and made a similar plate: it was nothing special.

Our server (not the big woman with bleached hair) was fine overall. I could tell she was tired, sad, or down in general. I felt sorry for her, but I also felt a little uncomfortable as I could tell she didn't want to wait on us. Another server took over for her at the end of our dining experience, and she was upbeat and attentive for the five minutes she waited on us.

As it ended up, we were at Hungry Cat for over and hour and a half. NO ONE came to sit at the "reserved" table during this time. 

Hungry Cat, service is important and on this you really fall short.

I'm not planning to go back here for dinner anytime soon.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Eos is an eclectic mixed crowd of "out of towners" and locals. Electronic music most times, but an all around good time. Strong drinks means management isn't cutting corners. The best bar-tending staff in town by far. Chill people and always aesthetically pleasing. I suggest Sat for the best representation of what they offer!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Diners | Review: Love Sunshine Cafe. They have a varied and extensive menu. Love the food. It is always fresh and well prepared. The staff is excellent. Friendly and attentive. Love this "local" cafe.

Categories: Food, Hawaiian, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, American (New) | Review: How I is awesome! Fresh, fabulous and great vibe. The shrimp burger and the fries are fantastic. Great location ( wish it was closer to LA) Don't miss the coconut donuts!!!!
Robin D

Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: I finally tried a bowl of noodle here, and it didn't live up to my expectation.  Good Sichuan cuisine can be so addictive with ample amount of numbing citrusy peppercorn and fragrant chili oil.  I admit the noodles were spicy and numbing.  There was also plenty of sodium, so it wasn't bland either.  What it greatly lacked was a good base made from simmering pork bones or chicken bones.  Imagine this without the spicy components, the broth is actually very watery.  I have nothing against MSG, I use it in my own cooking, but you can't rely only on MSG for umami.  The richness and savoriness of a well made stock can't be duplicated with a few sprinkles of MSG.  I also didn't like the beef being cut into perfectly square meat cubes.  The texture would be much better as thin slices.  If they can just make the stock better and tone down the MSG a little, the noodles here would be addictive.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Specialty Food, Japanese | Review: I'm literally sitting on the toilet, having a diarrhea now. I guess it can blame on the shintori because it is the only decent meal I've had today. (I had wonton bought from costco for lunch, but I was fine for the whole afternoon, and so is my boyfriend.)
(That's the first star you miss, causing me having a diarrhea.)
I and my boyfriend walked in shintori around 6:00pm today. It is small, but is a decent place. It got both tables and bar, the decoration was fulfill with Japanese stuff. Delicate and elegant. The paper lanterns they hanging on the top of the bar table got all those words written on. It was pretty cute. The restroom was super clean.
My boyfriend ordered a rainbow roll and 2pcs salmon sushi, the fishes were fresh. Also a beef bowl, I didn't try on the rice but I can tell they got a good chef depends on the beef and the way the manage with edamame(green soy bean), have them boiled and mixed with salt? I never seen anyone done that and it was super delicious and impressive.
I? On the other hand, I am a not that lucky person because I prefer food that are cold and raw. I ordered my recently favorite: 2pcs squid sushi, I hardly doubted that they forgot to take out the membrane of the squid, my tongue told me that.
(Another star you miss)
2pcs of octopus sushi, it is not fresh, it is not easy to cut it with your teeth, it supposed to taste very smooth and have the sweet unique taste as an octopus but it tasted like some weird, overnight, unable-to-chew, meat...It reminds me the look of toad...
(Another star you miss)
2pcs of scallop: raw, mixed with crab meat and mayonnaise, scallion on top, it is fine, but not stunning. Honestly, the scallion is a bit ruined it, and I'm not a big fan of raw scallop.
2pcs of mussels sushi, never heard that in other sushi restaurant, so I decided to give it a shot. Chefs had the mussels fried and put a good taste sauce on top, it should be shintori sauce, but they place a drop of chilli sauce on it, and I'm not a fan of spicy food.
Both scallop and mussels sushi, their seaweed wrap were not crunchy enough...My tongue guess is either they are about to expire or place in a cabinet for so long.
(Another one...)
First stop by at shintori, never gonna be there again, because of their not that fresh sushi and it caused a diarrhea.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: How incredible was this place?  Both the food and the service were incredible.  Though they were out of the wine we ordered, the waiter recommended a comparable but more expensive bottle for the same price.  That was our introduction to Bouchon.

It keeps getting better.  I have to recommend the Kurobuta Pork Belly with red cabbage and tater tots because it tastes like butter with heaven on it.  I could have eaten two of these.  The Trio of Scallops were seared just perfectly, and all of the accompaniments were surprising and delicious.  Service was perfect, and the atmosphere was cozy and elegant without being too stuffy.

Dessert was the real surprise, because you have to order the Blackberry-Lemon Upside-Down Cake.  Just trust me.  If I am ever in this area again, I cannot wait to go back here.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Chinese, Restaurants | Review: Not good, I just ate and my stomach and intestines are already 'complaining', IF you know what I mean. Pork dumplings were OK and that was the best thing available. They weren't in a hurry to refill them either,  so they knew. BTW, I'm reviewing their buffet only. No way I'd eat here again.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, French, Coffee & Tea, Bakeries, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: This place was very small but very cozy. We had to wait about 20 minutes for our sandwiches but it was worth the wait ! It was light and delicious! The chef who is also the owner came out to check with us and make sure everything was going good which added to the experience ! They have been open for only 6 months! I say check the place out !

Categories: Mexican, Vegan, Restaurants, Vegetarian | Review: Upon entering this place smells delicious. The Mexican vegan food is excellent with large portion sizes. We had the nachos and tacos. Wow !! Best nachos I have ever ordered. The tacos were also excellent. There's six unique kombuchas on tap brewed by the owner. You can have them mixed with unique lemonades like lavender or turmeric lemonade. Highly recommend if you are craving delicious Mexican food. You won't leave hungry.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: Parking can be tough but these tacos are so worth it! The Triple Play is the best deal. I've tried all 10 tacos on their menu, and the only one that I didn't thoroughly impress me was the grilled fish. Spicy crispy shrimp, bahn mi, Gangnam style, and ahi poke are my favorites! Hole-in-the-wall snack stand location next to the batting cages. Only outdoor seating

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: My favorite mexican place in SB! The salsas were amazing! I could simply eat their chips and salsa all day! The sopes and burrito were excellent. Kiddos loved the food too.

Categories: Bakeries, Food Trucks, Food, Cupcakes | Review: The cupcakes are moist and easy to swallow, but the price is a bit harder...

I tried the cupcake flight, which was an aesthetic/tastebud delight. 6 mini cupcakes are boxed in a half-dozen egg carton. How creative and cool is that? I shared this with a friend, so each of us got literally 3 bites worth of cupcake. But each bite was so good! Every cupcake was super sweet but not overwhelmingly so (maybe because we didn't have that much of it). Each cupcake also had their own distinct flavor as well, and in addition to delicately pipped frosting on top, there was also a bit of a filling. I particularly enjoyed the chocolate blackberry syrah cupcake. The fruity flavor, while sweet, helped tone down the heaviness of everything else. The blueberry blood orange chardonnay was also really nice. It reminds me of a tart, lemon flavored cupcake, but wayyyy better.

If money doesn't concern you, this is a cute, hip place to satisfy your sweet tooth!

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: Love that this restaurant exists and relieved to hear it was just a recent rumor about closing! All the food I've had here has been excellent and they are able to accommodate substitutions/ requests which is great. The staff is both attentive and knowledgeable which makes the experience enjoyable. Good bartender too and they all seem to,have awareness of the customer and their needs. Always a pleasant experience. Thank you, Oliver's!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Such great employees working tonight August 6th! Eric Ramono was amazing I asked him for an altering menu item an he was positive sweet and amazing, he was only nice !  Please treat these people amazing they are so nice!!!!!  Not only did he make sure he got my order right he actually made sure it was made/delivered correct! He was very sweet and customer attentive and very determined at his job.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Yummy! I've loved this place since the first time I discovered it while down here for work. This time had my family with me and they really enjoyed the casual feeling and good food. 

Great for a nice quick bite with kids.

Categories: Tapas Bars, Nightlife, Salad, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wine Bars, Cafes, Pizza, American (New) | Review: My husband and I originally intended to go to Wine Cask, but ended up in Intermezzo.
This place has a cool vibe, great wait staff and food to die for.
We are definitely coming back!   Meeting friends from LA here to catch up, drink a little and eat little. Can't wait, this place is awesome.
Yeah, the crispy cauliflower is so good, crazy since I hate cauliflower, but theirs is so yummy, a must try!

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: So I don't know how this place gets good reviews and the only thing is it's locals who have lost their taste for good Italian or tourist who go when they are super hungry.  This place was very average.  Moved here a year ago and we have tried this place twice hoping the 2nd time would be better.  Still average.  Tried the Pumpkin Ravioli with cream sage parmesan.  The whole dish was super sweet with brown sugar crumbs on top.  This should have been a dessert.  I also ordered a Cab Sauvignon and for such a quality restaurant the waiter should have at least made a suggestion that this meal would be very sweet.  Didn't go well together.  My husband got the Gargenelli Bolognese and it was not tasty at and sweet as well.  Seems like this chef like to make his food sweet.  Had the crab cakes and it was better than the rest.

Categories: Italian, Salad, Pizza, Restaurants, Sandwiches | Review: A hole in the wall with a handful of tables to accommodate dine-in. The pizza was very good with the right amount of cheese and sauce. The crust was light and crispy with a little bit of chew which was fantastic. We noticed a fair amount of calzones being ordered. They looked delicious. Will have to try that next time.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Excellent food/ presentation, and the staff is very attentive.
 
I've been going here for the last few years and every time I go they change little things here and there to make the experience even better.

I get better service here than some of the more expensive places down town.

Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: The beer was great and so was the food! This place gets packed so you should get there as early as possible if you want a seat!

Categories: Grocery, Specialty Food, Food, Health Markets, Local Services, Community Service/Non-Profit | Review: I like how they offer an abundant amount of healthy food options, BUT 1)they are very overpriced, sprouts is a way better alternative 2) they sold me 2 overly expired items and didn't accept the return Bc they neglected to give me a receipt (let it be known that I caught on to the expiration date  within that same hour so the same people saw me)

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Barbeque, Food, Sandwiches, Wine Bars, Diners, Bars, Specialty Food, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: Carefully designed dishes and a relaxed yet upscale atmosphere await at Barbareño!

I personally consider the cost at this restaurant to be a splurge for a special occasion, but if that's what you're looking for, then I recommend it.  Both indoor and outdoor seating areas are nice, and the professional service featured explanations about the special ingredients in each dish and frequent check-ins with us.

The appetizer Chef's Plate was a great start that included beef tartare crackers, shot glasses of avocado gazpacho rimmed with bee pollen (interesting!) and eggamuffins, which are soft pancakes topped with whipped egg whites and grated egg yolk.  All three were delicious.

The chicken wings were some of the best that I've ever had, period.  Highly recommended.

Although we were already satisfied at this point, we also shared the Santa Maria tri-tip.  This was also delicious and came with a generous helping of beans with a deconstructed pico de gallo.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Awesome causal one-off Mexican style restaurant!!    Grilled chicken--perfect!  Excellent restaurant-made roasted tomato/chile salsa.  Fresh made chips, REAL whole beans (not from a can).

How often do you see Beef Ribs on a menu?  Jerry's Pollofino's does them up right also....easy to eat off right the bone.  The ribs are a bit pricy but So worth it. Evrything else on the menu is a great deal.

Portions of nachos and burritos are NOT small!!

I'll go here every time I'm visiting the area.

Thanks Jerry for making some killa chow.  Keep it up.

Categories: Pizza, Italian, Fast Food, Restaurants | Review: Thank you so much for all that you are doing to stay open through all of the changing and Covid virus that we all are enduring. I am truly looking forward to enjoying State street as it once was and finding you open after 1 am so to get fed really good pizza and salads. I am glad they allowed you to have outdoor seating too. I'll be coming back up to have more pizza this week. An hour and 10-minute drive but it's worth every mile. You and your crew are always polite, and the quality of everything down to your salad dressing is amazing. Please stay open as quality family recipes such as yours are really hard to find.

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: This spot has amazing food and service. A great experience all around and I'd suggested it to anyone. We were welcomed in with open arms and it felt like home before we even sat down. Much appreciation to Denise and Harold the chef who made it a truly stand out experience!

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Coming fro NJ to SB- just walked past Finney's!!! Walked in ordered 4 apps and went to town!!! So amazing will definitely be coming back food and atmosphere are AMAZING... Presentation as well!

Categories: Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Pizza, Italian | Review: One of the worst places to eat in IV. I went here maybe.. twice? Each time, I was absolutely disgusted by the pizza.

I realize that the vast majority of the business for this place is drunk crowds of young people who may or may not be students (I suspect mostly out-of-towners and CC kids), and that their taste in food and well, everything in life is diminished by their intoxication, but there's still no excuse for eating here.

I'm not a fan of Woodstock's, but it's a better option. Pizza My Heart is like 20x the better option and Blaze Pizza just blows this place out of the water - although I don't think they serve beer. Get yourself some Blaze and get plastered before or after. Don't bother with Giovanni's.

Every time I walked past this place and it was crowded, I was always smh.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Lots of employees, very minimal customer service. Food was only so-so. Pretty disappointed. With our party of five, at 1 in the afternoon, we expected better!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: One of my favorite places to eat here in SB.  The Pad Thai San Road with tofu is delicious, duck buns are tasty as well.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Fast Food | Review: I am a fan of chipotle but will no longer go to this location as the portions are terrible. They give you a half scoop of meat. If I'm getting a chicken burrito I actually want to taste some chicken.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, American (New) | Review: Came here for a few drinks while I was waiting for happy hour with a friend.  I got the mojito with coconut milk I believe. OMG it was AMAZING, bartenders were cool, I may be back here today.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: I ordered the small Fish & Chips, and enjoyed. It was the right size for how hungry I was.  Good crunch on the bread, but the fish was a bit too soft. No salt came on the fries, but there should have been, or at least some sort of spice. Mac himself was there running the show: taking orders, handing out food, etc.

Categories: Bubble Tea, Restaurants, Taiwanese, Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: This was my first time here and they were extremely helpful. I was able to try onebeofre I got my actual drink. I needed up getting a taro drink that was amazing. The drinks are on the pricey side but def worth it. This is a hangout spot for groups. They were playing loud music and had a whole bunch of board games for you to choose from. They also have food options as well if you get hungry/connected to a restaurant.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: I'm downright obsessed with this ice cream. The lemon marionberry is a little piece of heaven and the salted caramel chip is amazing. The others are ok but not as good as these two. I just wish they had one in San Diego!

Categories: Restaurants, Specialty Food, Do-It-Yourself Food, Seafood Markets, Food, Caterers, Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, Event Planning & Services | Review: The fries were SO good! Fried cod tacos were good, hit the spot. What I was really impressed with was the level of customer service - thank you to all of the staff. You are truly terrific!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: So my sister and were looking for a New Mexican restaurant to try yesterday as we usually go to Los Agaves but the only item we really like there is the ceviche. We were craving those street tacos that are delicious but not every place can make them well. We liked up Aaron Sanchez recommendations in SB and came across Monys. What a great find. We got there around 3 and there was a line and all seats were taken as seating is limited. But people are eating and leaving so up to you to wait for a table. I'm glad we took the small one inside next to the fabulous salsa bar. One was better than the other they had like 8 of them. Peanut salsa was my favorite it had a nice kick to it and went great on the tacos we ordered. Tried al pastor Asada and carnitas. Pastor was our favorite although all 3 were delicious. Loved the marination and flavors. The pineapple added a little something extra to the meat. The tortillas were fresh and had chewy texture. My kind of tortilla not the one that falls apart when you start biting into taco. Also the price was super reasonable like $1.75 each. They give you chips and salsa on the house. Staff was friendly and efficient. Can't wait to come back here. Definitely worth the drive from LA. So this place is in the Funk zone of SB but what was interesting that no local could tell us why or how it got its name but we found out how expensive the new condos are going for. Mucho mucho dinero!!!$$$$$$ 
Two thumbs up for Monys!!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: It was my friends birthday and we wanted to go somewhere upscale, trendy but relaxed. Paid for Parking in back.  They seated us in a window seat in the front.  Gorgeous day with life going by,  it was perfect!  We started off with a a cocktail and we had ordered our main meal at the same time.  We were going to a concert at the bowl. Bread and butter came out quick along with our drinks.  
 I had the herb filet with mashed potatoes and a yummy cream mushroom sauce. My friend had the skirt steak with roasted potatoes. Both of our meals were served with green beans.  Everything was prepared perfectly!  The food was delicious.  The servce was quick and the staff very friendly

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Super rude service. When I made an reservation, theres lots of noise in their side and they cannot hear me clearly, after I tired two times, they just hung up my phone... wordless

Categories: Chinese, Restaurants | Review: Too much hype. Came here a couple of times. Place is loud, service mediocre,  food tasteless.  Decor needs an update. Hot tea was luke warm, food took a long wait, the chicken dishes are bland. Not recommended.

Categories: Chinese, Restaurants | Review: So disgusting that we stopped eating after 2 bites and had to get food somewhere else. The orange chicken was soggy and tasted like it was leftovers put under a heat lamp for HOURS. No crunchiness and just disgusting. The veggie egg rolls tasted like old seaweed and I spit it out immediately. How does a chinese restaurant mess up ORANGE CHICKEN???

Categories: Steakhouses, Local Flavor, Nightlife, Fish & Chips, Restaurants, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: We've been here twice and both times with a TravelZoo coupon; 1 shared appetizer, choice of soup or salad per person, 2 entrees, a bottle of wine and a shared dessert. Right when you walk in the hostess greets you with a warm and welcoming smile. We've sat in the back and in the front dining room. Both good, it depends if you want privacy or be in the middle of the action. I highly recommend their cioppino! It has a large array of seafood (different fish and shell fish) with yummy crusty garlic bread on top.  And save room for Mom's Apple pie! The biggest and most delious apple pie I have ever eaten! (luv u grandma, but their's is better)

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Coffee Roasteries | Review: I've passed by this place a million times and finally decided to stop in last week. I played it safe and ordered a vanilla latte. I got it iced, to go. It does take quite a bit longer for them to make the drinks than at a Starbucks, for obvious reasons, but the taste is definitely worth it! So much higher quality than I was expecting.

It was such a rich, lovely latte that I will definitely go back to order an enjoy it hot and in an actual mug when I'm not in a rush. The vibe of the shop is also wonderful. Very calm and comforting. Will definitely be going back soon!

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Fathers Day Review and first time @ BlueWater 

1. Reserved table for upstairs and outside @1pm. Upon arrival we were told they did not have our table and we would be seated inside. The most frustrating part of this is that they had a walk-in (no reservation) who requested outside and they were placed in front of us if we were willing to wait '2 hours' for the outside seating. 

2 Upon seating we were immediately told many things were no longer available including- ponzu scallops, mahi  mahi, and various small accompaniments like whipped cream. (Keep in mind they had only been open for one hour at this point) 

3. The 'Fishermens Bloody Mary' which was meant to be served with special condiments did not come with condiments as they weren't available. (And we were not made aware prior to it being served to the table)

4. An hour into service and after appetizers, we were told the  king crab legs we ordered were not available. 

5. Surf and Turf Steak was ordered medium rare and was served rare. The thing was still mooing. We returned it and they brought it back (reheated) grey and dry. We opted out of the steak completely. 

6. We ordered the freshly baked chips and were served fries. 



By the end of service and between the 4 of us, we had two successful plates served but still somehow managed to spend $125 on a mediocre meal.  ($155 with tip as it wasn't our waiters fault, and we actually felt bad hearing him, hesitantly, tell every single table they were out of almost half the menu.)

An overall disappointment for what was meant to be a special Fathers Day Lunch
Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: A bit more expensive than regular Mexican food, but totally worth the price. One meal was enough for two and both choices were delicious.

Categories: Health Markets, Coffee & Tea, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, American (New), Specialty Food | Review: Backyard Bowls has the best acai bowls. I love how they are served in cute and colorful glass bowls. Try their island bowl!!

Categories: Fast Food, Food, Acai Bowls, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants | Review: My friend Nyjee just recommended this place since we were in Santa Barbara today taking our engagement photos. Ever since his days at UCSB,  this is one of his must-stops when he goes back to SB. 

It was so delicious!! We ordered 5 drinks: Cranberry, Orange, Tropical Mango, Mocha and Peanut Butter. I always like fruity type and I'm not a fan of peanut butter---- but you have to try it!! It was so good... Just like all the other drinks.

Categories: Salad, Sandwiches, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Chicken Wings | Review: I had been waiting to try out Wingman Rodeo off Milpas since it had opened. One way or another, it was almost always not during their business hours. Nonetheless, I am one happy man since they happened to open up even closer to home in Old Town Goleta. Since Old Town Coffee opened their doors, I swore Old Town was on its way of becoming the "new, hip" place to live, dine, work in the Greater Santa Barbara area. Now that Wingman Rodeo is there, I am certain the area is changing--and for the better. Now all this section of Hollister Ave needs is some fresh paint!

After partaking in an Old Town Coffee Community Trivia night, myself and two others stopped in during their opening week. Thanks to the banner I knew they were open with hours late into the night (Mon-Sun til 10/10:30PM)! The space is currently sparse due to Covid restrictions, however, it had a few socially distanced tables. There was also an odd-looking machine with a lifesize drum similar to what you'd see at a raffle. The cashier said that it is going to be used to coat the wings in sauces. Neat!

The fried chicken here either comes in wing form, sandwich, burrito or on a salad. Then you choose your sauces/rubs with a whole line up of choices: Buffalo, atomic (when I say hot, I mean they're HOT with ghost peppers), pineapple bourbon, chipotle lime, sichuan tingly rub, Korean BBQ, rodeo parm, thai peanut, Cali BBQ, lemon pepper rub, and strawberry arbol. Since I am on a vegetarian kick, they offer the meat alternative of cauliflower. HOLY MOLY! The fried cauliflower tasted just as good, if not better, than fried chicken. I went with the Cali Burrito with Cali BBQ and was one happy camper!

I look forward to come back and try what else they have to offer.

Note: Since this is fried food it obviously tastes best fresh. So either dine in or take it to go yourself instead of using a delivery service. It's not fair to give them a negative review due to this fact with the complaint that the fries or chicken aren't crispy or warm. If you did order online for pick-up, you may need to wait since Wingman Rodeo wants it made fresh so you can enjoy it to the max.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: Went here with a group of people from work.  I had only eaten here once before.  The food is tasty and portions are not stingy.

Staff were friendly.  The owner was especially attentive, and when he discovered that half of our group (not including me, natch) had unimaginatively ordered the chicken tikka masala, he brought out a few other dishes on the house to let us sample the range of their wares.  The chicken chetinada was very good, a dish I'd never had in any Indian restaurant before.

That said, the place is not perfect.  The naan leaves something to be desired.  Especially the stuffed naans were a bit too doughy, almost mushy, although the plain and garlic naans were okay.

Also, just from a logistical perspective, I don't care for the way the food is served.  The rice, lentils, and vegetables are served in separate compartments of a large plastic tray.  These trays are really big and cumbersome and just look and feel really awkward when they're on the table.  They're too big for the amount of food they contain.  I would prefer to just have the rice on the plate, or on a separate ordinary plate/bowl.  It may sound weird, but I think this is actually one of the biggest negatives about the place for me.  Even though the food is good, these big, blocky, black plastic trays make you feel like you're eating in a cheap food court.  They should just give you regular plates with a separate serving dish for the entree.

Although you order at the counter, prices don't seem noticeably lower than at other local Indian restaurants that offer table service.  On the other hand, no table service means you don't have to tip so much, so you save in the end.

As always, my heart belongs to a different Indian restaurant, but India Club is a good choice.  All of the other Indian restaurants in the area are in or near downtown Santa Barbara, so if you want Indian food in Goleta this is definitely the place to go.  I also hear their lunch buffet is a good deal, and if it's like what we got for this dinner, I believe it.

Categories: Breweries, Brewpubs, Food | Review: A great place to hang out, have a simple meal, and enjoy a beverage. I came from Ventura County and was not expecting the city to be still under Covid restrictions (no indoor dining). So most places having ordering at the bar. 

My wife and I split a panini and and order of jalapeño poppers along with a few drinks. 

The air was warm, breeze smelt of the sea, and it was quiet enough that we could relax for a few hours without the kids. Honestly, my experience was nothing short of paradise. 

Great food and atmosphere.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Good but not great.  The ciopinno was solid and the service was great.  Bloody Mary and the wine list were also strong.  A great place for small kids because they are drowned out by the open kitchen. 

The garlic clams were breadier than I expected.

Skip the mild flavored clam chowder and bread basket.

Categories: Health Markets, Coffee & Tea, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, American (New), Specialty Food | Review: Overall very good experience. Diversified, tasty, food portions, nice deco... would give 5 if a bit more space to sit.

Categories: Gelato, Food, Specialty Food, Desserts, Chocolatiers & Shops | Review: My entire experience exceeding all expectations! The chocolates are absolutely one of a kind and so beautiful. I almost didn't want to eat them!!  A young man named Jordan was my first contact when I placed an order! He was so pleasant and helpful! You are fortunate to have someone that is so respectful and represents your image with such sincerety! I will be calling to order again soon and look forward to speaking with Jordan again!

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: By far the best Chicken Sandwich you will ever have!!! Other then that the environment is nice and drinks are decent!!! Service was top notch. My only gripe was wait time was predicted to be 15 min and we ended up waiting 35 minutes. But for the best chicken sandwiches in the world it's totally worth it!!!

Categories: Gelato, Food, Specialty Food, Desserts, Chocolatiers & Shops | Review: Fancy chocolates. Probably great for a Valentine's day gift but not an every day expenditure for me. Looked about $15 for 4 pieces but I'm sure it comes in amazing packaging. I could never make a chocolate as beautiful as these so don't get me wrong, well worth the up-spending for decadence. We opted for Gelato. It was great. Seating for us as well. Our favourite flavors of gelato were Strawberry Basil, Pistachio, and Chocolate Chip Cookie.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: We went here for lunch, and it was worthwhile. The lunch specials are cheap-ish ($6-$8), but we all went for the lunch buffet, which was $10.

The cheese enchiladas and the chimichangas were greasy, but a-ok, the chimichangas receiving especially good marks. The carnitas--my standard order--were solid. The best dish, by far, was the chicken enchiladas; there were legitimately good. I also liked that they had a soup and garnishes. It was just a flavorful broth with some vegetables, but it really added a lot to the meal.

The food wasn't spicy, which was good for us, as my fiancee has trouble with heat. I simply added hot sauce as needed.

Categories: Pakistani, Restaurants, Buffets, Seafood, Halal, Indian, Vegetarian, Vegan, Food, Salad, Desserts | Review: had it at home after picking up on 4th of july. I mean what else says America than some chicken Tika? food was great.. i like it alot. Its not like walking on pioneer in Artesia, but its great.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: I like this place a lot.  Excellent lattes, nice decor, super pleasant people behind the counter. The furniture is clean and comfortable.  Nice patio.  Definitely a big step up from Mojo which previously occupied the same spot!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: When I showed up to Casa Blanca i was thrilled with the promptness that the chip and salsa boy displayed. It was as if we sat down, BAM, chips. With 4 differnent salsas. The service was very good. My only complaint was when I went to the bathroom, near the back, I saw a manager yelling at a nice (hopefully legal) hispanic boy. I think her name was Dodi, or Dede or something of that nature. No Yelling Dede!!!1 Meanie Weanie

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: NOT WORTH IT!!! Ice cream is under average and very expensive (small scoops)... !!! $3.25 for a very tiny single scoop and $7.25for a triple
Scoop which was more like a single ..... Was very disappointed ...GO TO RITE AID just a few steps across the street on the same side..!!!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: This past Sunday at about 3pm we were seated. The waitress took our order,we had drinks and were enjoying the view of the ocean , l noticed Thestop by our table he did l told him the waitress disappeared finding out she never put our order in.
The manager was a total zero, Didn't no how to handle a simple problem.Without having a great location the food was a c and the serv

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: A favorite of "locals". Great team-service and Great Food.
Try the Smoked-Salmon Flatbread. Better than Spago.
The Calamari Appetizer is massive (big enough to share).
Love the Linguini with Fresh Clams.Delicious. Follow
with the Tangerine Sorbet on a sweet crisp. Yum.
Lively, Welcoming Vibe!

Stephen S

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: We ordered several dishes but the worst was panang curry with salmon. The salmon was coated in something we've never eaten. It was like jelly but sticky and made you feel like you were eating slimy fat. It was disgusting.  None of the 5 dishes impressed us. We ordered Gyoza as an appetizer. We expected normal size gyoza but instead for $8 received miniature size which means a slither if chicken inside and mostly breading

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Nightlife, New Mexican Cuisine, Restaurants | Review: The service was awesome! I'm a big fan of great customer service and they have it! Great place great music, and yes great food!  I will be coming back here again soon!  I forgot to mention the drinks are very good also.
Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: As a self professed foodie. I've never had a bad experience here! Drinks appetizers  muffins fooooood :)

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: I've been here twice now and every time I leave completely satisfied. The ceviche is out of this world. The pork belly quesadilla is insanely good. Also if you're looking for a delicious yet spicy drink, get the Dragon's Breath! This place is so cute and the food is amazing.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: Great drinks, and service. Our special.. chili rellanos was nothing special.  Come for drinks,& service not  for food

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Gastropubs, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: The staff was perfect, I can see them putting in their best effort into giving excellent customer service while a smile! (To my fellow bipoc, you don't get any sense of prejudice at all, you know how at some nicer, and predominantly white restaurants and servers, you get that, I did not see or feel that here at all)

I came for drinks and appetizers to hang out, it wasn't busy, so I didn't feel rushed at all, and everything was delicious! 

I couldn't give it perfect stars because the furniture was a bit sticky, especially under the arm rests, and sides of the tables, and the pigeons... I know you can't really control them, but the pigeons were feeding off of food scraps on the floor, so maybe if the floors were swept very regularly, it might mitigate the pigeons dive bombing past your shoulders.

Categories: Nightlife, American (Traditional), Cafes, Restaurants, Wine Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, Bars | Review: Came here on a Sunday night and it was about what I would expect:  service was a little off (asked for silverware a couple times), food wasn't quite right (turkey burger was undercooked and left me with anxiety about salmonella afterwards).  To their credit, they took back the turkey burger and did everything to make me happy, so I appreciated that.  Highlights: their turkey club is really good, above average....the key is the thick sliced home roasted turkey, bacon which is super crisp, and the wheat toast is nicely toasted.  My mom likes the pot roast and gets it every time.  They've survived this location for years, when others that came before have failed, which is a testament to their ability to serve up stable, consistent neighborhood meals for locals who want a fairly predictable meal without cooking at home.

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: I always enjoy eating here! The part I like most is consistency. I come here and always order the same thing, and it always tastes the same! As it should! I have tried most of their ramens and have to say the thin noodle "Kuro" is my favorite. I prefer the pork belly, but sometimes order pork shoulder to make me feel better about what I am eating. 

Food aside, service is great most of the time, waiters arrive promptly to your table to get your drink orders, drinks come fast and you're ready to order! Food usually comes between 5-10 minutes depending on order complexity and people at your table. 

Come here on a busy night, and tables may be a bit awkward for your comfort. Tables are booth/chair style and sometimes you get placed right in the middle of two large groups. If you are an introvert like me, make sure to request a table not smooshed between others.... well worth the wait!

Overall, I really hope this restaurant stays for a long time! It's a great spot to get your ramen fix, or try a quality ramen for the first time!! Try it! You won't be sorry....

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: This place is incredible. I would recommend the onion soup and the duck. Service is exceptional. A must-try when in Santa Barbara.

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Wineries, Food, Wine Tasting Room | Review: I walked into Frequency on a Saturday afternoon looking for a quick couple bottles to take to a wedding party I was attending and was immediately taken by the decor. Bailey was remarkably attentive and quite knowledgeable about varietals. She introduced me to the Grenache (outstanding) as well as the Syrah and a few others all the while effortlessly attending to the other customers filing in. Great product and excellent service; one would be hard pressed to find its equal.

Categories: Fast Food, Burgers, Coffee & Tea, Restaurants, Food | Review: I constantly have to check my food because I ask for no Lettuce on my mcchickens  but yet I get tons of it and there tropic berry sprite is always just soda water

Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Himalayan/Nepalese | Review: My boyfriend took me there today and the food was absolutely amazing. The service was great, very friendly and there was some music playing in the background which added a nice cultural touch. We will definitely add this restaurant to our list of favorites and to the list of restaurants that we recommend to our friends.

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Food, Vegan, Cafes, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Gluten-Free, Vegetarian, Acai Bowls | Review: Highly recommend this place!! Loved the veggie stir fry and skinny greens juice  thank you so much Marika for helping us and even writing us a list of things to do in the areas, as we were new to Santa Barbara!

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Dawn Patrol was a late minute breakfast choice and it didn't disappoint!  Had the 3 egg frittata w/ half of a seeded avocado and it was so good.  Just enough food and the chai tea was delicious.  Hubbie had the hash w/ sweet potato and chicken and he loved it.  If you are in Santa Barbara, near state street it is a definite break spot.  Dog friendly and mimosas!

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: Came here Sunday night at 5:30pm, it was pretty quiet. Service was very good, and fast! I loved the oyster Rockefellers - plump, sweet oysters with cheese and some spicyness to them. Not impressed with the hot pot - the seafood was fresh and abundant, but the broth tasted like buttery hot water -  very underwhelming. I abandoned it and ordered the Salmon Tacos instead- great move! For other reasons I've stopped eating tacos for awhile, and these ones looked so good in the pics I decided to give them a try. Glad I did - They brought me back on to the taco wagon! And for the mediocre hotpot, they gave me three more Rockefellers on the house. Great service! Really liked this place would recommend

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: I consider myself an ice cream specialist.  I eat lots of ice cream! Very picky on the brands I will buy.  In the age of trendy restaurants with unique fusions of food styles and flavors I guess ice cream wants in too.  This spot had unique flavor combinations using flavors of the season.  My favorite was the mixed berry cobbler.  I went back for seconds! Be prepared to wait in line.  This place is not a secret!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants | Review: After a disappointing meal at the harbor, we came back to the hotel to get some drinks and nosh on appetizers. The mojito I got was good and strong. Bartender didn't skimp on the alcohol for sure! The ahi poke and chocolate molten cake we ordered were both pretty good. The next morning we had breakfast at Bistro 1111 again. It was a little on the pricey side, but the food was good.

Categories: Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Sandwiches, American (New), Tapas Bars, Wine Bars | Review: Ok so this wine bar just very well may be the best venue in Santa Barbara. Locally owned and operated, it has a fantastic wine selection and great light food menu. They often have a live guitarist who is off-the-chart amazing, playing Spanish-style- flamenco variety music.  (forgive me I'm not an expert in music genre names) We were in a group, and it was the perfect ambiance and sound level to talk and enjoy the music. It would be a great place to take a date. Highest recommendations.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Ordered the Chowder Fries, House made Chowder (New England), and Steak Frites. All were bomb. Service was excellent. View outside was incredible.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Wonderful view! Friendly staff and good food.  I love brunch here. Crap cakes are great! Nice to be right on the water. If there's a wait you can just relax and watch the dogs play on the beach.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: This is what I like to call casual fast food. It's better than your typical fast. Food establishment but also twice the price. The food is good, not exceptional. I'd eat here more often but it's a bit pricey for my taste. Example. My family of 3 spends $30 here, but can eat at Lillie's tacos for under $20, we can have Thai for $40 or chipotle for $25..... So when we come here I really have to ask myself if it's worth the extra money and carbs.......

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: Went for a bachelorette dinner with 7 ladies on Friday 3/3 around 8:30-9 had an amazing experience.  Our server friendly, knowledgeable and very accommodating with requests! (Unfortunately, I can't remember his name!) I went to Ladies room, but the busser had taken my delicious guava margarita before I had finished it and without hesitation, I was provided with a new one! The food was delicious!  My gf and I shared the short rib and Brussels, so good!  The other ladies all seemed very pleased with their meals too! The ambiance and decor is very nice as well!  Our server even offered to take our group photo was very nice to take a couple for us before wishing us a good night!  Prices aren't bad either, for the quality of what you get, well worth it.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: Great service, great hookah, great wine and most importantly great food! We had hummus with shawarma, salmon with basil cream sauce, mashawi combo and baklava..all were perfectly cooked and seasoned. We had outdoor seating which was comfortable with the fire pit in the middle of the table, also great music and a lively belly dancer. Good times were had by all!

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Just walking up and down State st. Looking for a nice place to eat. We picked Aldo's cause the patio looked so cozy, and it was. I'd come back again next time we pass through town. The sauce with the mussels was amazing! We also had crab cakes, scallops and a fettucini. The only thing negative I could say is I didn't love my wine choice, the house Pinot. Our waitress was great too, very soft spoken but attentive.  Ill be back.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Best Nutella crepe I've had in my life! The crepe had a light airy crisp, lighter than the usual crepes I've had in the past. It had a perfect balance of Nutella and whipped cream, done asian-style and not too sweet. Great service.

Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Indian | Review: Tasty food but too oily. The ambience was pleasant and calm.

Food
We ordered the paneer Kadhai, garlic naan, tandoori roti and veg Biryani
The veg kadhai and biryani tasted excellent. But unfortunately they both had loads of oil at the bottom of the serving dish
I was surprised the tandoori roti was without salt. When I asked the server to check with the chef they said that's how it's supposed to be.
I have never ever had roti without salt before so maybe something for them to think about

Service 
We were seated immediately and the order was taken on time. But after the first order was taken we had to try really hard to get the servers' attention.
Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Nightlife, Restaurants, Seafood, Cafes | Review: One of the few places open late in Santa Barbara. Nothing fancy. But nice. Decent selection.

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Brazilian, Restaurants, Food, Salad, Acai Bowls | Review: Passed by this restaurant on State street and decided to have lunch here. The beef stroganoff and the grilled fish were super delicious!  Would definitely come back here when I visit Santa Barbara again!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Latin American, Mexican, Seafood | Review: My husband and I LOVE this place!! Best tacos in town! So delicious... get the tacos el pastor! They're amazing :)

Categories: Italian, American (New), Restaurants | Review: It was ok.  I went there last week with a group of 4.  Like others have said, good thing we had a reservation.  If you like "gamey" meats then you will enjoy some of their offerings.  We had about 8 different things off the menu, apps and entrees.  I went with the bacon pizza as the other items were a little fancy for my taste.  Everyone at the table said my pizza had the most flavor of any dish they tried that night.  Which is kind of sad given they ordered some fancy stuff.  Our service was ok, I waited over 30 minutes for a sprite as I was not drinking like the other 3 at my table.  They did however comp an entree that one person did not enjoy, which was nice.  My party said they had a nice wine list.  I think they need to work some kinks out...has potential, but given the hype I was expecting more.  If I liked the type of food they serve, I would have given 3 stars.  But for the $$, there are other restaurants in SB I would rather enjoy.  I will give it one more chance in about a month so they can work on some things.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Lazy worker shut down store 6 mins early on a Saturday. Sign still says open and I ask her what time do you close. 

She answers at 9. 

I reply it's 8:54pm I still have 6 mins. 

Sorry we're closed. 

- way to hire motivated employees Rainbow -
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Best veggie burritos in town. Fresh vegetables and well-seasoned rice. Not sure if it's worth $13 a burrito, but will be back for sure to.. double check?

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, American (New) | Review: Thank you Yelpers! I literally typed in "romantic dinner" near our SB hotel's address 15 minutes before we were due to take off and Blue Agave popped up. We figured we'd go with the flow but made reservations just in case we didn't see a place we particularly wanted to eat at...plus we wanted to make sure wherever we ate was approved of by Yelpers. ;) I left a message with the restaurant and they called me back a few hours later to confirm.

Located off State Street, it's a darkly lit, romantic restaurant and bar. It was surprisingly empty and we were seated immediately downstairs by the front door. A bit on the loud side for it to be REALLY romantic, but the atmosphere was spot on.

For starters we ordered the Black Tiger Shrimp and the hand cut fries. The shrimp was amazing, served on top of foccacia bread and a realllyy yummy garlic sauce that we later used as dipping sauce for our table bread. We both ordered the filet mignon (cuz we were starving and craving red meat after all that wine we ingested earlier in the day) which was tendered and delicious. The pureed potatoes were also really good, though we both thought the creamed spinach was way too salty.

We had a great time here though and probably would have stayed to drink at the bar if we didn't have three bottles of wine waiting for us in the  hotel room. ;)

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: Good coffee. I had a cappuccino royale and loved it. Only one girl working but I got my order right away since there was no wait even though many people were there. Comfortable sitting room with interesting local art. Looks like they have a nice selection of teas too.

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Bluewater Grill is one of my favorite places in Santa Barbara for a great view and to enjoy fantastic seafood. Richie at the bar upstairs is hands-down One of the best bartenders.

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza | Review: We felt like pizza and this hit the spot! Reading the reviews we could see that most bad reviews were for customer service. We kept our order pretty simple. We just wanted pizza. I ordered the Garden pizza with pepperoni on have. I wanted the whole thing Garden with pepperoni on half but got half Garden , half pepperoni. Didn't bother complaining. The young man who took our order didn't speak very good English. I had to have him repeat everything he said. And he obviously didn't understand me either.
The pizza was delicious.
This is not a date night restaurant, very casual. Good for groups( it's large) and kids.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Ended up driving up here after returning from SF and having major seafood cravings. Yes, our craving was that bad. We had a large bowl of clam chowder, a bowl of clams, calamari and fish&chips. Our eyes were bigger than our stomachs but luckily we were able to finish all of the yummy goodness!

This place really satisfied my cravings and definitely far exceeded my expectations. We came during lunchtime on a weekday and it was still super packed. We ended up waiting about 30 minutes and were then sat outside. There was a cover protecting us from the rain and also heaters so we were perfectly content. It was actually nice to watch the drizzle come down while we ate. 

They give generous portions here so you wont leave hungry. The staff is very nice. Definitely one of the spots I will be returning to on my next trip to SB.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: LOVE this place. Love the new location. Parking can be tricky, but well worth it. This small restaurant has a great menu, the yellow curry is my favorite, but everything is good! AND they do take out! Sooo good. :)

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Always good. Have been going for a couple of years -- the chef/owner is incredibly nice. The seafood ingredients are fantastic and fresh. Salsas are delicious. It can be a bit chaotic, but always worthwhile.  The molcajetes is great, as were the seafood stuffed chiles.

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: My boyfriend and I were in town visiting family and were desperately in need of boba. So we decided to check this place out. We went two days in a row, and the first day I ordered a peach passion tea with their brown honey boba (since they don't offer normal boba) and lychee jelly. I got my drink and came to the realization that it was missing the lychee jelly. I was peeved but it wasn't a big enough deal for me to have them remake my drink, everyone makes mistakes it was no big deal. My boyfriend got a honey milk tea with honey boba and it was fine.
But when we went in the next day I decided to get a strawberry banana smoothie with honey boba instead. And when I got my drink the taste of artificial banana overpowered the entire drink. It tasted as if they dumped powdered banana flavoring into my drink added some pink dye in and handed it to me. It was so bad that I couldn't even drink it. And my boyfriend again ordered a honey milk tea and it tasted completely different than the day before, almost as if they forgot to sweeten the drink. I know they're a new establishment but I don't think that constitutes terrible drinks. Also the customer service wasn't too great. Needless to say, I won't be returning any time soon.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: I'm not happy about writing this review.  My husband I and went to two different restaurants in SB over our anniversary and were disappointed by both of them.  This was the second one.  We thought there was no way it could be worse than the other.  It seems no one in SB can put together a good Cioppino!
We had a wonderful Ceviche appetizer and the specialty drinks were great!  We were so excited for the Cioppino.  We asked the server NOT to split the soup b/c we like to share.  They did anyway, thinking they were doing us a favor.  Instead the portions were so unbalanced one of us got a big bowl of Campbell's tomato soup and the other got a bowl of overcooked and undersized seafood.  The soup made me wish for a side of grilled cheese, not seafood.
So once again, please, eat here for anything and probably everything else.  Can someone please find me some good Cioppino?!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, French | Review: I cannot believe I haven't written a review for Julienne yet. What a oversight!

We've been coming to Julienne for a few years now, even before we moved back up to SB. We love Justin and Emma, and how they run their restaurant. The food! Oh, the food.
