Justin is a creative chef without being weird for the sake of being weird, as seems to happen most of the time nowadays, in restaurants of this caliber. There's rarely anything on the menu I wouldn't try, even if it is a little outside my comfort zone.

The dishes are flavorful without relying on heavy sauces, and the ingredients are superb--mainly because Justin makes it himself when he can. I see him down at the farmer's market all the time, which makes us happy that he's supporting our local farmers. 

Highlights of the menu: Always get his charcuterie--he's probably made it himself. The jerk octopus is my all-time favorite. Pig's ear salad with egg. Mussels with merguez sausage and fennel broth.

I also took one of Justin's cooking classes, and I was very impressed. He's a natural in front of a crowd, but more importantly, he gives you real recipes. I would recommend taking one if you want to learn a few tricks of the trade--I'm an avid home chef, but I still learned a lot!

With Julienne in town, we have a taste of the LA/SF/NYC caliber dining without the ridiculous wait for reservations or pretentious chef or service. We bring as many people to Julienne as we can (and everyone has loved it!), and we are personally very happy that they renewed their lease for another 5 years.

Thanks guys! Keep up the awesome work!

Categories: Wine Bars, Arts & Entertainment, Wine Tasting Room, Nightlife, Bars, Food, Wineries | Review: Awesome place, awesome service. Best chard in town. Dave took care of us and was very personable. We have been to many tasting rooms in the funk, this is by far the best vibe.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: Check the prices on Postmates and then use Postmates as a menu. Called in the order and decided to pick it up myself. All the prices were different and always on the more expensive. The Side salad was free according to Postmates for the fried noodles and the house salad was only a two dollar add on to the fish and chips according to post-mates and everything else was a few dollars more. So you can see different prices all round, If I would've known I would've not eaten here.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Wow.  Amazing ice cream. Be sure to try the ice cream sandwich. Your choice of homemade cookie and ice cream combination. Ice cream produced from grass fed milk.  Would be 5 out of 5 but it's quite expensive.  Worth the splurge.

Categories: Restaurants, Ethiopian, French | Review: This may be one of the easiest 5 star reviews I've ever had to give. Without a doubt it was one of the most phenomenal meals I've had in a very long time. 

We started off with the Mushrooom/bread appetizer with a balsamic vinegar "dressing" and a salmon, capered cream cheese delight. Paired with a nice glass of Chardonnay, it nicely enhanced the flavors of the perfectly sautéed mushrooms.

We shared the flank steak and lamb chops as the main course. All I can say is.... "WOW!" How did they do it? The meat slid off the bone as quickly as I slid out of my chair with happiness. I highly suggest getting the meat medium rare for the full, slide out of the chair effect.

The service was outstanding. The evening was such a great experience that I would go out of my way if in the area to make sure I get another meal here again.

5 stars all the way.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: I love to sit at this comfortable indoor/outdoor bar for a glass of wine before going to a movie across the street. Service is quick and friendly. The thin crust pizza is very good. I think it's in the top two for Santa Barbara pizzas I've had so far (with Olio Pizzeria -- Olio is more Italian style, Patxi more American style).

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: After waiting 15min for a glass of water at a different Santa Barbara Mexican restaurant, my girlfriend and I laughed saying "give me a water and we'll give a 5 star review!"

The Milpas location is a little different than the other SB "Los Agaves." Milpas is: order at the counter and they bring you your food. You do not have a waiter who you continuously order from. The salsa and soda bar are self serve. If you plan on having more than one beer, you will have to time the line correctly.

I ordered a Bohemia beer and "Aranchera" meat. Very tasty, not over cooked! The homemade tortillas were an added bonus!!

The salsa bar has a great variety of flavors and heat! We ordered a side of guacamole which was also fresh and tasty!

My girlfriend ordered a Dos Equis and "Alambres"... Her chicken was very well prepared. Not dry, great flavor! It came mixed with onions and bell peppers.

All in all, great lunch at under $40 for two with beer! 

Clean restaurant, food prepared as ordered in a timely manner.... And great flavor!!!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: Friendly service. Great breakfast, lots of asparagus and mushrooms in my asparagus omelette and rest of the family pleased as well. Dined in. Thoughtful Covid measures.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: This place is good. If your looking for a nice restaurant on the pier with a nice view look no further. I highly recommend if your just looking for a place with drinks and a view, mind you, you will have to pay excessive amounts for that drink that's just okay. 
But if your okay with paying more for a drink come here, I feel like the view is all that really makes this place attractive. The food is not good.

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Food, Vegan, Cafes, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Gluten-Free, Vegetarian, Acai Bowls | Review: Great salads, kombucha and the vegan brownie is moist and decadent.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: Totally impressed by this place! As a self diagnosed fish snob, the red grouper was amazing. My dad is a spear fisherman and the fish was just as fresh as he would bring home. Great service, very very reasonable prices. My new favorite place!!

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: So incredibly yummy and very friendly and accommodating staff. Anything you choose on the menu will be delicious! This was my third time and I will definitely be back. Thank you!

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: SOOO GOOOD. Took a trip to Cambria from LA, and needed to make a stop for a quick bite. This place is amazing, better than Baos i've had in LA. Ordered the PB&J, Pork Belly, and KFC. Excellent service too, everyone's so friendly. 10/10 recommend.
Categories: Ramen, Vegetarian, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: Great service, fast, VERY CLEAN, always friendly, easy takeout... one of my favorite spots, generous servings.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: DON'T GO HERE.  They are dishonest.  Bait and switch is the name of the game here.  They advertise a lobster special Mondays and Tuesdays.  So we went Tuesday at 6pm.  Sat down, ordered the lobster.  About 10 minutes after they brought us bread and drinks someone comes to the table to tell us "sorry, we are all out of lobsters."  This happened last time we went for lobster too.  This time we just up and walked out.  Not going to play their bait and switch game.  Besides, a noisy, rather unpleasant place.  Santa Barbara has such wonderful restaurants.  Unfortunately this is NOT one of them.  Do yourself and favor and go somewhere else!

Categories: Salad, American (New), Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: I don't like writing bad reviews but here goes! 

This restaurant use to be Fresco Café North, but has now turned over a new leaf and decided to change their name to "The Fig Grill". Same owners but a different name, and a revamped menu is the concept of this new change.

I checked out their menu on-line and they offer a good variety of choices such as hot/cold sandwiches, wraps, burgers, salads, and small plates (tapas). It all sounded so appetizing and I couldn't make up my mind but I opted to try the Turkey Cranberry hot sandwich. This comes with your choice of soup or salad.

The turkey sandwich consists of oven roasted turkey breast, caramelized onions, cranberry sauce, and brie cheese on sourdough. I ordered half a sandwich and salad for $8.95. 

Sounds great right? Well, sorry to say but this was not appetizing at all. The turkey tasted like it was about a week old, I didn't see or taste any brie, and the cranberry was too sweet for my taste. Everything was just wrong with this sandwich! I thought about taking it back and complaining but I didn't have much time being on my lunch break. 

The side salad was the only thing I ended up eating but it was lacking the balsamic vinaigrette. 

Tip: They do have Wi-Fi but you need to ask the cashier for the password.

Alcoholic Beverages: They offer Beer & Wine 

They do have a small kids menu and their dinner menu starts after 4:00pm. 

Prices are pretty reasonable here so I'm not totally writing off this restaurant. I hope my next visit will be a better experience so I can update this review.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, French | Review: We had a lovely meal at this cute restaurant over MLK weekend.  My sweetie and I had the tasting menu (four courses--with the wine pairings)  Lots of food so go hungry!  On reflection, we could have made do with the three course menu... but there were so many interesting things on the menu that we wanted to try everything!  As it was we were able to try eight different dishes on the menu between the two of us.  Everything is interesting, everything is delicious and the wine pairings were not run-of-the-mill wines.  (also, very generous pours!)  I loved it.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I went for the Pad Thai but decided to get something different and tried the salmon. It was amazing! I just wish they had the salmon on the delivery menu. Delicious!

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: If you're looking for traditional tapas, this is not the place , kust few tapas  with fancy names that are not representative of the true Spanish tapas , we also order the maricos paella , wow we were deeply dissapoointed . The place is cute and trendy but after we left we were looking for a place to eat . Read the menu carefully.
Categories: Nutritionists, Traditional Chinese Medicine, Health & Medical, Acupuncture | Review: Went here for some TCM (traditional chinese medicine), left me with severe stomach pain

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Was craving a quesadilla so decided to stop here since it was one of the only places with Mexican food still open. Such a disappointment, the quesadilla itself was not horrible but I have had better. Not only was it falling apart, but there was hardly any cheese and the "guacamole" they included on the side was super bland. Literally tasted like they just mashed up some avocados and left it like that. Idk if it was because it was 30 minutes before closing but the food wasn't all that great. I also asked for a horchata which she told me they didnt have, so then I asked for a jamaica and once again they didnt have that either and the girl ay the cashier said oh they didnt make them today....both items are on the drink menu so I find it kind of weird that they choose not to make it on certain days. The only reason I give this place 3 stars is because the girl was nice and gave me a free fountain drink.

Categories: Bars, Mediterranean, Restaurants, Nightlife, Moroccan, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Italian | Review: Intersting menu, food quality is pretty good but prices are high. Drink prices high too. Great views & a nice experience except our waitress Daniella was much bigger on attitude than on service. Tried to tell us there were too many reservations to fit us in when 2/3 of the tables were empty & res signs on just a couple. Same situation 40 min later as we checked out.

Categories: Steakhouses, Burgers, Bars, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: After experiencing a poor dinner the other night and only a mediocre lunch in the afternoon, I was pretty amazed by this restaurant. I had searched on yelp and both of those places for dinner and lunch had received many good ratings, but I beg to differ (I already wrote a review for each of those dining places). I randomly went into Holdren steak and it broke my scale. I got the 6 oz. filet mignon with the Rosemary blue cheese sauce. I'm not one that likes to try new things and especially when it has blue cheese in/with it, but that sauce was delicious. It was creamy and textured right, it totally balanced the meal out. I would like to note here that if you're not planning on using any of the sauces, the steak might be a little salty (to me it was). 

The service we got was A1 ;). We went in at around 8:30 on a Sunday evening and there was no wait. The waitresses were very friendly and helpful.

Overall, I really enjoyed this place and I would definitely recommend it to others.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Our last day in Santa Barbara and decided to have one of our last meals here. Wait time was reasonable about a 20 minute wait on a Saturday for the lunch time crowd. I ordered mimosas and the truffle burger with jalapeños. The food came out very quickly so fast I wasn't close to finishing my first drink lol. The burger and truffle fries were so delicious. Each bite was so savory and juicy. It's definitely worth visiting! We enjoyed our time.

Categories: Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches | Review: Whenever I am in Santa Barbara, this place is a must! Whether one is nursing a hangover, waking before a work day or just wants to relax and enjoy the sunshine...Daily Grind has the answer. 

The Daily Grind seems to be my remedy. I can trust that my Soy Haze will always be hot and ready when I need it and their muffins and bagel sandwiches will always satisfy my appetite. C cannot get over there smoothies. She recommends the Equinox. She calls it the perfect smoothie.

Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Himalayan/Nepalese | Review: We were staying in a hotel close by and in walking State Street found the Himalayan Kitchen.  While we have had Indian food before, we hadn't had Himalayan or Nepalese.

The seating is at tables with beautiful placemats on a white table cloth, covered by glass, with red cloth napkins beautifully folded (Bishop's Hat?).

Be sure to read the section at the beginning of the menu - about the chef (born at the foot of Mt. Everest, if I recall correctly).  I sooo wish they'd had a little page to take w/us about th chef and the food.

The menu is extensive - offereing soups, appetizers, salads, main dishes from vegetables, chicken, etc - soooo many things to read and from which to choose.

Each staff person who came to our table was SOOO nice and so pleased when we expressed appreciation for the meal/food.  We asked lots of questions before ordering - it was soooo hard to choose.  They were attentive.

They brought a VERY flat piece of bread with a surface that made me think of a sea anemone, along with a tomato based dipping sauce.

While we are vegetarian, I was intrigued by the offering of yak  - and the explanation that it was an authentic dish.  It was SOOOO good - (No, I do not remember the name of the dish I got -it's the first one under Yak on the menu!)  There was a broth, potatoes, etc.  

My husband got the first of the chef's specialties - yogurt marinated vegetable kebobs over sizzling onions.  It was very good. He also got lentil soup - VERY different than anything we've had (we eat lots of lentils) but so very good. We shared our meals, so got to experience several new flavors.

I cannot eat spicy foods, so asked for mild seasoning.  I really didn't taste any flavoring, so . . would maybe ask from "some" spice next time.  

If we lived in the area, we'd visit here often to try different menu items

Prices vary by what you get.  You can get soup very reasonably.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: If you're looking to have a group event, bring them to Sharkeez and get set up with Deena. She's an absolutely awesome chick and is able to hook your group up. She is always on point and has made my experiences at Sharkeez the best I've ever had at any restaurant or bar, EVER! Just yesterday I brought a group of about 15 people and she made sure we got a ton of free stuff (an appetizer, two bottles of champagne, and a bottle of Skyy), not to mention buy one get one for 25¢ drinks for everyone in my group for the entire 4 hours we were there! 

I can't emphasize enough how much she does and how awesome she is. She's always cheerful and helpful, and whenever you see her she's got a huge smile on her face. I even pulled her manager aside yesterday during our party and told him how awesome of an employee she is. I should have mentioned that whatever she's getting paid isn't enough because she's not only generating revenue for Sharkeez, but is creating long lasting customer relationships. Her help and service alone make me want to keep coming back to Sharkeez for all of my gatherings and parties. Cheers to you, Deena!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: By far my most loved mexican food restaurant of all time. Always fresh and full of flavor. the crunchy tacos... amazing.. the chille relleno burrito, out of this world.. chorizo breakfast torta... my mouth is watering just thinking about it. I've tried a good amount of the menu and I've never had one bad thing. And the staff is just as friendly and attentative as the food is wonderful. I adore this place and its definitely in my top 10 reasons why I would never move from santa barbara.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Had a really good time here. The dance floor was just big enough, and the music was just fun enough. The outdoor patio was nice, and I met a lot of good people there. Could have lived without having to wait in LINE to get out to the patio, but whatever - the drinks we strong.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, French, Vegan, Seafood, Asian Fusion, Gluten-Free, American (New) | Review: I convinced my mom we had to eat here so that I could enjoy a vegan dinner and she could have a meat dinner. It was one of the best meals of my whole life! It was crazy flavorful and perfect for everyone involved. A definite return trip is necessary! This place gives me the smiles.
